revision.comment: 新しいページ：鹿我別。
revision.text:
'''鹿我別'''（かがわけ<ref>読みは『日本古代氏族人名辞典』鹿我別項による。</ref>）は、[[古墳時代]]の[[人物]]。
[[上毛野氏|上毛野君]]の祖。[[神功皇后]]年間に[[朝鮮]]へ派遣されたという[[武将]]である。[[応神天皇]]年間に派遣された'''巫別'''（かんなぎわけ）は同一人物とされる<ref name="古代氏族人名辞典">『日本古代氏族人名辞典』鹿我別項。</ref>。
== 事績 == 『[[日本書紀]]』[[神功皇后]]49年3月条によると、[[荒田別]]とともに将軍に任じられ[[新羅]]征討に参加した。神功皇后50年2月条、帰国した旨が記される。
同書[[応神天皇]]15年8月条では、巫別が荒田別とともに[[百済]]に派遣され、翌年[[王仁]]を連れて帰っている。この「巫別」は鹿我別と同一人物とされる<ref name="古代氏族人名辞典"/>。この条において、荒田別・巫別は「上毛野君の祖」と記載されている。
『[[国造本紀]]』（『[[先代旧事本紀]]』第10巻）[[浮田国造]]条には、「[[成務天皇]]年間に崇神天皇五世孫・賀我別王が国造に任じられた。」と記載されている。
なお鹿我別の名は、『[[住吉大社神代記]]』でも新羅征討の説話において記載されている。
== 考証 == 鹿我別の存在については、『[[百済記]]』を原資料とする実在の人物であるのか、後世に追記された伝説上の人物であるか、説が分かれている<ref name="古代氏族人名辞典"/>。
また「かが」の読みから、[[下野国]][[足利郡]]（現 [[栃木県]][[足利市]]周辺）と関連づける説がある<ref>『日本地理志料』に記載（『毛野国の研究 古墳時代の解明 下』第三章四節より）。また、鹿我別は地名「足利」の由来の一説とされている（[http://crd.ndl.go.jp/reference/modules/d3ndlcrdentry/index.php?page=ref_view&id=1000047466 足利の地名の由来を知りたい。（栃木県立足利図書館回答）]（レファレンス協同データベース））。</ref>。
== 脚注 == {{reflist}}
== 参考文献 == * 『日本古代氏族人名辞典』（[[吉川弘文館]]、2010年普及版）鹿我別項
== 関連項目 == * [[毛野氏]] ** [[上毛野氏]]
{{DEFAULTSORT:かかわけ}} [[Category:古墳時代の人物]] [[Category:毛野氏]]

'''鹿我別'''（かがわけ<ref>読みは『日本古代氏族人名辞典』鹿我別項による。</ref>）は、[[古墳時代]]の[[人物]]。
[[上毛野氏|上毛野君]]の祖。[[神功皇后]]年間に[[朝鮮]]へ派遣されたという[[武将]]である。[[応神天皇]]年間に派遣された'''巫別'''（かんなぎわけ）は同一人物とされる<ref name="古代氏族人名辞典">『日本古代氏族人名辞典』鹿我別項。</ref>。
== 事績 == 『[[日本書紀]]』[[神功皇后]]49年3月条によると、[[荒田別]]とともに将軍に任じられ[[新羅]]征討に参加した。神功皇后50年2月条、帰国した旨が記される。

『[[国造本紀]]』（『[[先代旧事本紀]]』第10巻）[[浮田国造]]条には、「[[成務天皇]]年間に崇神天皇五世孫・賀我別王が国造に任じられた。」と記載されている。
なお鹿我別の名は、『[[住吉大社神代記]]』でも新羅征討の説話において記載されている。
== 考証 == 鹿我別の存在については、『[[百済記]]』を原資料とする実在の人物であるのか、後世に追記された伝説上の人物であるか、説が分かれている<ref name="古代氏族人名辞典"/>。

== 脚注 == {{reflist}}
== 参考文献 == * 『日本古代氏族人名辞典』（[[吉川弘文館]]、2010年普及版）鹿我別項
== 関連項目 == * [[上毛野氏]]

'''鹿我別'''（かがわけ{{Sfn|鹿我別（古代氏族）|1979年}}）は、[[古墳時代]]の[[人物]]。
[[上毛野氏|上毛野君]]の遠祖で、[[神功皇后]]の時に[[朝鮮]]へ派遣されたという[[武将]]である。[[応神天皇]]年間に派遣された「'''巫別'''（かんなぎわけ）」は同一人物とされる{{Sfn|鹿我別（古代氏族）|1979年}}。
== 記録 == 『[[日本書紀]]』[[神功皇后]]49年3月条によると、[[荒田別]]とともに将軍に任じられ[[新羅]]征討に参加した。神功皇后50年2月条、帰国した旨が記される。

『[[先代旧事本紀]]』「[[国造本紀]]」[[浮田国造]]条では、[[成務天皇]]年間に崇神天皇五世孫の「賀我別王」が国造に任じられたと記載されている。
なお鹿我別の名は、『[[住吉大社神代記]]』でも新羅征討の説話において記載されている。

revision.comment: ミス修正（古墳時代の人物→古代日本の人物）：文献上の人物を古墳時代という実時間の時代区分に載せるべきでなかった。

revision.text:
'''鹿我別'''（かがわけ{{Sfn|鹿我別（古代氏族）|2010年}}）は、『[[日本書紀]]』等に伝わる古代[[日本]]の[[人物]]。
[[上毛野氏|上毛野君]]の遠祖で、[[神功皇后]]の時に[[朝鮮]]へ派遣されたという[[武将]]である。[[応神天皇]]年間に派遣された「'''巫別'''（かんなぎわけ）」は同一人物とされる{{Sfn|鹿我別（古代氏族）|2010年}}。
== 記録 == 『[[日本書紀]]』[[神功皇后]]49年3月条によると、[[荒田別]]とともに将軍に任じられ[[新羅]]征討に参加した。神功皇后50年2月条、帰国した旨が記される。
同書[[応神天皇]]15年8月条では、巫別が荒田別とともに[[百済]]に派遣され、翌年[[王仁]]を連れて帰っている。この「巫別」は鹿我別と同一人物とされる{{Sfn|鹿我別（古代氏族）|2010年}}。この条において、荒田別・巫別は「上毛野君の祖」と記載されている。
『[[先代旧事本紀]]』「[[国造本紀]]」[[浮田国造]]条では、[[成務天皇]]年間に崇神天皇五世孫の「賀我別王」が国造に任じられたと記載されている。
なお鹿我別の名は、『[[住吉大社神代記]]』でも新羅征討の説話において記載されている。
== 考証 == 鹿我別の存在については、『[[百済記]]』を原資料とする実在の人物であるのか、後世に追記された伝説上の人物であるか、説が分かれている{{Sfn|鹿我別（古代氏族）|2010年}}。
『史略名称訓義』や『皇胤志』では荒田別の子に鹿我別王をあげるが、鹿我別の後裔の[[吉弥侯部]]一族から物部斯波連が賜姓されたことや、荒田別と鹿我別が同じ時代に活動したことから、実際には上毛野物部君の祖である[[景行天皇]]朝に活躍した[[夏花命]]がその父でないかと見る説がある。また浮田国造に任じられたという伝承から、その墓が[[宇多郡]]の[[桜井古墳]]に当たるのではないかとする説がある<ref>[[宝賀寿男]]「[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/hitori/kimikobe1.htm 吉弥侯部姓斑目氏の系譜]」『古樹紀之房間』、2016年。</ref>。
「かが」の読みから、[[下野国]][[足利郡]]（現・[[栃木県]][[足利市]]周辺）と関連づける説がある<ref>『日本地理志料』に記載（『毛野国の研究 古墳時代の解明 下』第三章四節より）。また、鹿我別は地名「足利」の由来の一説とされている（[http://crd.ndl.go.jp/reference/modules/d3ndlcrdentry/index.php?page=ref_view&id=1000047466 足利の地名の由来を知りたい。（栃木県立足利図書館回答）]（レファレンス協同データベース））。</ref>。
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}}
== 参考文献 == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=鹿我別|title=日本古代氏族人名辞典 普及版|publisher=[[吉川弘文館]]|isbn=978-4642014588|ref={{Harvid|鹿我別（古代氏族）|2010年}}}}
== 関連項目 == * [[上毛野氏]]
{{DEFAULTSORT:かかわけ}} [[Category:日本神話の人物]] [[Category:上毛野氏|伝かかわけ]] [[Category:古代日朝関係]]

「かが」の読みから、[[下野国]][[足利郡]]（現・[[栃木県]][[足利市]]周辺）と関連づける説がある<ref>『日本地理志料』に記載（『毛野国の研究 古墳時代の解明 下』第三章四節より）。また、鹿我別は地名「足利」の由来の一説とされている（[http://crd.ndl.go.jp/reference/modules/d3ndlcrdentry/index.php?page=ref_view&id=1000047466 足利の地名の由来を知りたい。（栃木県立足利図書館回答）]（レファレンス協同データベース））。</ref>。

『[[先代旧事本紀]]』「[[国造本紀]]」[[浮田国造]]条では、[[成務天皇]]年間に崇神天皇五世孫の「賀我別王」が国造に任じられたと記載されている。
なお鹿我別の名は、『[[住吉大社神代記]]』でも新羅征討の説話において記載されている。
== 考証 == 鹿我別の存在については、『日本書紀』や各種系図、『[[百済記]]』を原資料とする実在の人物であるのか、後世に追記された伝説上の人物であるか、説が分かれている{{Sfn|鹿我別（古代氏族）|2010年}}。
『史略名称訓義』や『皇胤志』では荒田別の子に鹿我別王をあげるが、鹿我別の後裔の[[吉弥侯部]]一族から物部斯波連が賜姓されたことや、荒田別と鹿我別が同じ時代に活動したことから、実際には上毛野物部君の祖である[[景行天皇]]朝に活躍した[[夏花命]]がその父でないかと見る説がある。また浮田国造に任じられたという伝承から、その墓が[[宇多郡]]の[[桜井古墳]]に当たるのではないかとする説がある<ref>[[宝賀寿男]]「[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/hitori/kimikobe1.htm 吉弥侯部姓斑目氏の系譜]」『古樹紀之房間』、2016年。</ref>。
「かが」の読みから、[[下野国]][[足利郡]]（現・[[栃木県]][[足利市]]周辺）と関連づける説がある<ref>『日本地理志料』に記載（『毛野国の研究 古墳時代の解明 下』第三章四節より）。また、鹿我別は地名「足利」の由来の一説とされている（[http://crd.ndl.go.jp/reference/modules/d3ndlcrdentry/index.php?page=ref_view&id=1000047466 足利の地名の由来を知りたい。（栃木県立足利図書館回答）]（レファレンス協同データベース））。</ref>。
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}}
== 参考文献 == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=鹿我別|title=日本古代氏族人名辞典 普及版|publisher=[[吉川弘文館]]|isbn=978-4642014588|ref={{Harvid|鹿我別（古代氏族）|2010年}}}}
== 関連項目 == * [[飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧]] * [[上毛野氏]]

revision.comment: 修正
revision.text:
{{基礎情報 公家 | 氏名 = 鹿我別 | 画像 = | 画像サイズ = | 画像説明 = | 時代 = [[古墳時代]] | 生誕 = 不明 | 死没 = 不明 | 改名 = | 別名 = 鹿我別命、巫別、巫別命、賀我別命、賀我別王 | 諡号 = | 神号 = | 戒名 = | 墓所 = | 官位 = [[浮田国造]] | 主君 = [[成務天皇]]、[[神功皇后]]、[[応神天皇]] | 氏族 = [[上毛野氏|上毛野君]]・[[下毛野氏|下毛野君]]同族、[[吉弥侯部]]祖 | 父母 = 父：[[大荒田別命]]<ref name="nakata">[[中田憲信]]『皇胤志』（[[宝賀寿男]]「第2章 皇族系氏族 第8節 毛野氏族 1上毛野君」『古代氏族系譜集成』上巻、古代氏族研究会、1986年、558、559頁。）。</ref>（[[#考証|後述]]） | 兄弟 = [[現古命]]<ref name="nakata"/>、[[竹葉瀬命]]<ref name="nakata"/>、[[奈良別君]]<ref name="nakata"/>、[[田道君]]<ref name="nakata"/>、[[賀表乃眞稚命]]<ref name="nakata"/> | 妻 = | 子 = | 特記事項 = }} '''鹿我別'''（かがわけ{{Sfn|鹿我別（古代氏族）|2010年}}、[[生没年不詳]]）は、『[[日本書紀]]』等に伝わる[[古墳時代]]の[[豪族]]で初代'''[[浮田国造]]'''。[[上毛野氏|上毛野君]]・[[吉弥侯部]]の遠祖で、[[神功皇后]]の時に[[朝鮮]]へ派遣されたという[[武将]]である。[[応神天皇]]年間に派遣された「'''巫別'''（かんなぎわけ、かがわけ）」は同一人物とされる{{Sfn|鹿我別（古代氏族）|2010年}}。'''鹿我別命'''、'''賀我別王'''とも。
== 記録 == 『[[日本書紀]]』[[神功皇后]]49年3月条によると、[[荒田別]]とともに将軍に任じられ[[新羅]]征討に参加した。神功皇后50年2月条、帰国した旨が記される。
同書[[応神天皇]]15年8月条では、巫別が荒田別とともに[[百済]]に派遣され、翌年[[王仁]]を連れて帰っている。この「巫別」は鹿我別と同一人物とされる{{Sfn|鹿我別（古代氏族）|2010年}}。この条において、荒田別・巫別は「上毛野君の祖」と記載されている。
『[[先代旧事本紀]]』「[[国造本紀]]」[[浮田国造]]条では、[[成務天皇]]年間に崇神天皇五世孫の「賀我別王」が国造に任じられたと記載されている。
なお鹿我別の名は、『[[住吉大社神代記]]』でも新羅征討の説話において記載されている。
== 考証 == 鹿我別の存在については、『日本書紀』や各種系図、『[[百済記]]』を原資料とする実在の人物であるのか、後世に追記された伝説上の人物であるか、説が分かれている{{Sfn|鹿我別（古代氏族）|2010年}}。
『史略名称訓義』や『皇胤志』では荒田別の子に鹿我別王をあげるが、鹿我別の後裔の[[吉弥侯部]]一族から物部斯波連が賜姓されたことや、荒田別と鹿我別が同じ時代に活動したことから、実際には上毛野物部君の祖である[[景行天皇]]朝に活躍した[[夏花命]]がその父でないかと見る説がある。また浮田国造に任じられたという伝承から、その墓が[[宇多郡]]の[[桜井古墳]]に当たるのではないかとする説がある<ref>[[宝賀寿男]]「[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/hitori/kimikobe1.htm 吉弥侯部姓斑目氏の系譜]」『古樹紀之房間』、2016年。</ref>。
「かが」の読みから、[[下野国]][[足利郡]]（現・[[栃木県]][[足利市]]周辺）と関連づける説がある<ref>『日本地理志料』に記載（『毛野国の研究 古墳時代の解明 下』第三章四節より）。また、鹿我別は地名「足利」の由来の一説とされている（[http://crd.ndl.go.jp/reference/modules/d3ndlcrdentry/index.php?page=ref_view&id=1000047466 足利の地名の由来を知りたい。（栃木県立足利図書館回答）]（レファレンス協同データベース））。</ref>。
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}}
== 参考文献 == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=鹿我別|title=日本古代氏族人名辞典 普及版|publisher=[[吉川弘文館]]|isbn=978-4642014588|ref={{Harvid|鹿我別（古代氏族）|2010年}}}}

revision.text:
{{基礎情報 公家 | 氏名 = 鹿我別 | 画像 = | 画像サイズ = | 画像説明 = | 時代 = [[古墳時代]] | 生誕 = 不明 | 死没 = 不明 | 改名 = | 別名 = 鹿我別命、巫別、巫別命、賀我別命、賀我別王 | 諡号 = | 神号 = | 戒名 = | 墓所 = | 官位 = [[浮田国造]] | 主君 = [[成務天皇]]、[[神功皇后]]、[[応神天皇]] | 氏族 = [[上毛野氏|上毛野君]]・[[下毛野氏|下毛野君]]同族、[[吉弥侯部]]祖 | 父母 = 父：[[御諸別命]]または[[大荒田別命]]<ref name="nakata">[[中田憲信]]『皇胤志』（[[宝賀寿男]]「第2章 皇族系氏族 第8節 毛野氏族 1上毛野君」『古代氏族系譜集成』上巻、古代氏族研究会、1986年、558、559頁。）。</ref>（[[#考証|後述]]） | 兄弟 = 大荒田別命、[[市入別命]]または[[現古命]]<ref name="nakata"/>、[[竹葉瀬命]]<ref name="nakata"/>、[[奈良別君]]<ref name="nakata"/>、[[田道君]]<ref name="nakata"/>、[[賀表乃眞稚命]]<ref name="nakata"/> | 妻 = | 子 = | 特記事項 = }} '''鹿我別'''（かがわけ{{Sfn|鹿我別（古代氏族）|2010年}}、[[生没年不詳]]）は、『[[日本書紀]]』等に伝わる[[古墳時代]]の[[豪族]]で初代'''[[浮田国造]]'''。[[上毛野氏|上毛野君]]・[[吉弥侯部]]の遠祖で、[[神功皇后]]の時に[[朝鮮]]へ派遣されたという[[武将]]である。[[応神天皇]]年間に派遣された「'''巫別'''（かんなぎわけ、かがわけ）」は同一人物とされる{{Sfn|鹿我別（古代氏族）|2010年}}。'''鹿我別命'''、'''賀我別王'''とも。
== 記録 == 『[[日本書紀]]』[[神功皇后]]49年3月条によると、[[荒田別]]とともに将軍に任じられ[[新羅]]征討に参加した。神功皇后50年2月条、帰国した旨が記される。
同書[[応神天皇]]15年8月条では、巫別が荒田別とともに[[百済]]に派遣され、翌年[[王仁]]を連れて帰っている。この「巫別」は鹿我別と同一人物とされる{{Sfn|鹿我別（古代氏族）|2010年}}。この条において、荒田別・巫別は「上毛野君の祖」と記載されている。
『[[先代旧事本紀]]』「[[国造本紀]]」[[浮田国造]]条では、[[成務天皇]]年間に崇神天皇五世孫の「賀我別王」が国造に任じられたと記載されている。
なお鹿我別の名は、『[[住吉大社神代記]]』でも新羅征討の説話において記載されている。
== 考証 == 鹿我別の存在については、『日本書紀』や各種系図、『[[百済記]]』を原資料とする実在の人物であるのか、後世に追記された伝説上の人物であるか、説が分かれている{{Sfn|鹿我別（古代氏族）|2010年}}。
『史略名称訓義』や『皇胤志』では荒田別の子に鹿我別王をあげる。
宝賀寿男は、上毛野物部君の祖である[[景行天皇]]朝に活躍した[[夏花命]]がその父でないかと主張した<ref name="kimikobe">[[宝賀寿男]]「[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/hitori/kimikobe1.htm 吉弥侯部姓斑目氏の系譜]」『古樹紀之房間』、2016年。</ref>。「国造本紀」には[[崇神天皇]]の五世孫とあり、これに従えば鹿我別は荒田別と同世代になる。
浮田国造に任じられたという伝承から、その墓が[[宇多郡]]の[[桜井古墳]]に当たるのではないかとする説がある<ref name="kimikobe"/>。
「かが」の読みから、[[下野国]][[足利郡]]（現・[[栃木県]][[足利市]]周辺）と関連づける説がある<ref>『日本地理志料』に記載（『毛野国の研究 古墳時代の解明 下』第三章四節より）。また、鹿我別は地名「足利」の由来の一説とされている（[http://crd.ndl.go.jp/reference/modules/d3ndlcrdentry/index.php?page=ref_view&id=1000047466 足利の地名の由来を知りたい。（栃木県立足利図書館回答）]（レファレンス協同データベース））。</ref>。
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}}
== 参考文献 == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=鹿我別|title=日本古代氏族人名辞典 普及版|publisher=[[吉川弘文館]]|isbn=978-4642014588|ref={{Harvid|鹿我別（古代氏族）|2010年}}}}
== 関連項目 == * [[飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧]] * [[上毛野氏]]
{{DEFAULTSORT:かかわけ}} [[Category:日本神話の人物]] [[Category:古墳時代の人物]] [[Category:古代日本の人物]] [[Category:陸奥国の人物]] [[Category:福島県の歴史]] [[Category:国造]] [[Category:上毛野氏|伝かかわけ]] [[Category:古代日朝関係]]

contributor.username: ja>学者気取りの先頭はここで終わり！

『[[先代旧事本紀]]』「[[国造本紀]]」[[浮田国造]]条では、[[成務天皇]]年間に崇神天皇五世孫の「賀我別王」が国造に任じられたと記載されている。

== 考証 == 鹿我別の存在については、『日本書紀』や各種系図、『[[百済記]]』を原資料とする実在の人物であるのか、後世に追記された伝説上の人物であるか、説が分かれている{{Sfn|鹿我別（古代氏族）|2010年}}。
「かが」の読みから、[[下野国]][[足利郡]]（現・[[栃木県]][[足利市]]周辺）と関連づける説がある<ref>『日本地理志料』に記載（『毛野国の研究 古墳時代の解明 下』第三章四節より）。また、鹿我別は地名「足利」の由来の一説とされている（[https://crd.ndl.go.jp/reference/modules/d3ndlcrdentry/index.php?page=ref_view&id=1000047466 足利の地名の由来を知りたい。（栃木県立足利図書館回答）]（レファレンス協同データベース））。</ref>。
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}}
== 参考文献 == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=鹿我別|title=日本古代氏族人名辞典 普及版|publisher=[[吉川弘文館]]|isbn=978-4642014588|ref={{Harvid|鹿我別（古代氏族）|2010年}}}}
== 関連項目 == * [[飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧]] * [[上毛野氏]]
{{DEFAULTSORT:かかわけ}} [[Category:日本神話の人物]] [[Category:古墳時代の人物]] [[Category:古代日本の人物]] [[Category:陸奥国の人物]] [[Category:福島県の歴史]] [[Category:国造主]][[Category:上毛野氏|伝かかわけ]] [[Category:古代日朝関係]]

revision.comment: 戻し：「国造主」という学術用語なし。勝手な造語につき。
revision.text:
{{基礎情報 公家 | 氏名 = 鹿我別 | 画像 = | 画像サイズ = | 画像説明 = | 時代 = [[古墳時代]] | 生誕 = 不明 | 死没 = 不明 | 改名 = | 別名 = 鹿我別命、巫別、巫別命、賀我別命、賀我別王 | 諡号 = | 神号 = | 戒名 = | 墓所 = | 官位 = [[浮田国造]] | 主君 = [[成務天皇]]、[[神功皇后]]、[[応神天皇]] | 氏族 = [[上毛野氏|上毛野君]]・[[下毛野氏|下毛野君]]同族、[[吉弥侯部]]祖 | 父母 = | 妻 = | 子 = | 特記事項 = }} '''鹿我別'''（かがわけ{{Sfn|鹿我別（古代氏族）|2010年}}、[[生没年不詳]]）は、『[[日本書紀]]』等に伝わる[[古墳時代]]の[[豪族]]で初代'''[[浮田国造]]'''。[[上毛野氏|上毛野君]]・[[吉弥侯部]]の遠祖で、[[神功皇后]]の時に[[朝鮮]]へ派遣されたという[[武将]]である。[[応神天皇]]年間に派遣された「'''巫別'''（かんなぎわけ、かがわけ）」は同一人物とされる{{Sfn|鹿我別（古代氏族）|2010年}}。'''鹿我別命'''、'''賀我別王'''とも。
== 記録 == 『[[日本書紀]]』[[神功皇后]]49年3月条によると、[[荒田別]]とともに将軍に任じられ[[新羅]]征討に参加した。神功皇后50年2月条、帰国した旨が記される。
同書[[応神天皇]]15年8月条では、巫別が荒田別とともに[[百済]]に派遣され、翌年[[王仁]]を連れて帰っている。この「巫別」は鹿我別と同一人物とされる{{Sfn|鹿我別（古代氏族）|2010年}}。この条において、荒田別・巫別は「上毛野君の祖」と記載されている。
『[[先代旧事本紀]]』「[[国造本紀]]」[[浮田国造]]条では、[[成務天皇]]年間に崇神天皇五世孫の「賀我別王」が国造に任じられたと記載されている。
なお鹿我別の名は、『[[住吉大社神代記]]』でも新羅征討の説話において記載されている。
== 考証 == 鹿我別の存在については、『日本書紀』や各種系図、『[[百済記]]』を原資料とする実在の人物であるのか、後世に追記された伝説上の人物であるか、説が分かれている{{Sfn|鹿我別（古代氏族）|2010年}}。
「かが」の読みから、[[下野国]][[足利郡]]（現・[[栃木県]][[足利市]]周辺）と関連づける説がある<ref>『日本地理志料』に記載（『毛野国の研究 古墳時代の解明 下』第三章四節より）。また、鹿我別は地名「足利」の由来の一説とされている（[https://crd.ndl.go.jp/reference/modules/d3ndlcrdentry/index.php?page=ref_view&id=1000047466 足利の地名の由来を知りたい。（栃木県立足利図書館回答）]（レファレンス協同データベース））。</ref>。
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}}
== 参考文献 == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=鹿我別|title=日本古代氏族人名辞典 普及版|publisher=[[吉川弘文館]]|isbn=978-4642014588|ref={{Harvid|鹿我別（古代氏族）|2010年}}}}

revision.comment: Created page with "{{Infobox Ancient Aristocrat | Name = Kagawake | Image = | Image_Size = | Image_Caption = | Era = [[Kofun period]] | Birth_Date = Unknown | Death_Date = Unknown | Alternate_Names = Kagawake no Mikoto, Kannagiwake, Kannagiwake no Mikoto, Kagawake no Ō, Kagawake no Mikoto | Posthumous_Name = | Deity_Name = | Dharma_Name = | Burial_Place = | Titles = [[Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko|First Ukita Kuni no Miyatsuko]] | Allegiances = [[Emperor Seimu]], [[Empress Jingū]], [..."
revision.text:
{{Infobox Ancient Aristocrat | Name = Kagawake | Image = | Image_Size = | Image_Caption = | Era = [[Kofun period]] | Birth_Date = Unknown | Death_Date = Unknown | Alternate_Names = Kagawake no Mikoto, Kannagiwake, Kannagiwake no Mikoto, Kagawake no Ō, Kagawake no Mikoto | Posthumous_Name = | Deity_Name = | Dharma_Name = | Burial_Place = | Titles = [[Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko|First Ukita Kuni no Miyatsuko]] | Allegiances = [[Emperor Seimu]], [[Empress Jingū]], [[Emperor Ōjin]] | Clan = {{ill|Kamitsuke Clan|ja|上毛野氏}}, {{ill|Shimosuke Clan|ja|下毛野氏}}, Ancestor of the {{ill|Kibiya Clan|ja|吉弥侯部}} | Parents = | Spouse = | Children = | Notable_Features = }} '''Kagawake''' (かがわけ{{Sfn|Kagawake (Ancient Clan)|2010}}, birth and death dates unknown) is a historical figure from the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} referenced in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} and other historical texts. He was the first '''{{ill|Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|浮田国造}}''', an ancestor of the {{ill|Kamitsuke Clan|ja|上毛野氏}} and {{ill|Kibiya Clan|ja|吉弥侯部}}. Kagawake is described as a general sent to the Korean Peninsula under {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}'s reign. The "Kannagiwake" (巫別) mentioned during the {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} period is believed to be the same individual{{Sfn|Kagawake (Ancient Clan)|2010}}.
== Records ==
The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} records Kagawake's involvement in the following events: * In the 49th year of {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}, he was appointed a general alongside {{ill|Aratawake|ja|荒田別}} to subjugate {{ill|Silla|ja|新羅}}. His return to Japan is noted in the 50th year. * In the 15th year of {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}, "Kannagiwake" was dispatched with Aratawake to {{ill|Baekje|ja|百済}}. The following year, they returned with {{ill|Wani|ja|王仁}}. "Kannagiwake" is identified with Kagawake{{Sfn|Kagawake (Ancient Clan)|2010}}. This record also describes Aratawake and Kannagiwake as "ancestors of the Kamitsuke Clan." * According to the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}, "Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi" section, "Kagawake no Ō," a fifth-generation descendant of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}, was appointed as the Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko under {{ill|Emperor Seimu|ja|成務天皇}}'s reign.
The name Kagawake also appears in the {{ill|Sumiyoshi Taisha Jindaiki|ja|住吉大社神代記}} in the context of the Silla expedition.
== Analysis ==
Kagawake's existence is debated among scholars. Some argue he was a historical figure based on the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, genealogical records, and references in the {{ill|Baekje Annals|ja|百済記}}. Others suggest he is a later legendary addition{{Sfn|Kagawake (Ancient Clan)|2010}}.
The phonetic similarity of "Kaga" has led to theories linking Kagawake to {{ill|Ashikaga District|ja|足利郡}}, present-day {{ill|Ashikaga|ja|足利市}}, Tochigi Prefecture. Some traditions suggest Kagawake is connected to the origin of the place name "Ashikaga." <ref>{{Cite book|title=Japanese Geographic Resources|chapter=Research on Keno Province in the Kofun Period}}</ref>
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== References == * {{Cite book|title=Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Clans: Popular Edition|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館}}|year=2010|chapter=Kagawake|isbn=978-4642014588|ref={{Harvid|Kagawake (Ancient Clan)|2010}}}}

The phonetic similarity of "Kaga" has led to theories linking Kagawake to {{ill|Ashikaga District|ja|足利郡}}, present-day {{ill|Ashikaga|ja|足利市}}, Tochigi Prefecture. Some traditions suggest Kagawake is connected to the origin of the place name "Ashikaga." <ref>{{Cite book|title=Japanese Geographic Resources|chapter=Research on Keno Province in the Kofun Period}}</ref>

{{Infobox Ancient Aristocrat | Name = Kagawake | Image = | Image_Size = | Image_Caption = | Era = [[Kofun period]] | Birth_Date = Unknown | Death_Date = Unknown | Alternate_Names = Kagawake no Mikoto, Kannagiwake, Kannagiwake no Mikoto, Kagawake no Ō, Kagawake no Mikoto | Posthumous_Name = | Deity_Name = | Dharma_Name = | Burial_Place = | Titles = [[Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko|First Ukita Kuni no Miyatsuko]] | Allegiances = [[Emperor Seimu]], [[Empress Jingū]], [[Emperor Ōjin]] | Clan = {{ill|Kamitsuke Clan|ja|上毛野氏}}, {{ill|Shimosuke Clan|ja|下毛野氏}}, Ancestor of the {{ill|Kibiya Clan|ja|吉弥侯部}} | Parents = | Spouse = | Children = | Notable_Features = }} '''Kagawake''' (かがわけ{{Sfn|Kagawake (Ancient Clan)|2010}}, birth and death dates unknown) is a historical figure from the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} referenced in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} and other historical texts. He was the first '''{{ill|Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|浮田国造}}''', an ancestor of the {{ill|Kamitsuke Clan|ja|上毛野氏}} and {{ill|Kibiya Clan|ja|吉弥侯部}}. Kagawake is described as a general sent to the Korean Peninsula under {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}'s reign. The "Kannagiwake" (巫別) mentioned during the {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} period is believed to be the same individual{{Sfn|Kagawake (Ancient Clan)|2010}}.
== Records ==
The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} records Kagawake's involvement in the following events: * In the 49th year of {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}, he was appointed a general alongside {{ill|Aratawake|ja|荒田別}} to subjugate {{ill|Silla|ja|新羅}}. His return to Japan is noted in the 50th year. * In the 15th year of {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}, "Kannagiwake" was dispatched with Aratawake to {{ill|Baekje|ja|百済}}. The following year, they returned with {{ill|Wani|ja|王仁}}. "Kannagiwake" is identified with Kagawake{{Sfn|Kagawake (Ancient Clan)|2010}}. This record also describes Aratawake and Kannagiwake as "ancestors of the Kamitsuke Clan." * According to the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}, "Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi" section, "Kagawake no Ō," a fifth-generation descendant of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}, was appointed as the Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko under {{ill|Emperor Seimu|ja|成務天皇}}'s reign.
The name Kagawake also appears in the {{ill|Sumiyoshi Taisha Jindaiki|ja|住吉大社神代記}} in the context of the Silla expedition.
== Analysis ==
Kagawake's existence is debated among scholars. Some argue he was a historical figure based on the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, genealogical records, and references in the {{ill|Baekje Annals|ja|百済記}}. Others suggest he is a later legendary addition{{Sfn|Kagawake (Ancient Clan)|2010}}.
The phonetic similarity of "Kaga" has led to theories linking Kagawake to {{ill|Ashikaga District|ja|足利郡}}, present-day {{ill|Ashikaga|ja|足利市}}, Tochigi Prefecture. Some traditions suggest Kagawake is connected to the origin of the place name "Ashikaga." <ref>{{Cite book|title=Japanese Geographic Resources|chapter=Research on Keno Province in the Kofun Period}}</ref>
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== References == * {{Cite book|title=Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Clans: Popular Edition|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館}}|year=2010|chapter=Kagawake|isbn=978-4642014588|ref={{Harvid|Kagawake (Ancient Clan)|2010}}}}

The phonetic similarity of "Kaga" has led to theories linking Kagawake to {{ill|Ashikaga District|ja|足利郡}}, present-day {{ill|Ashikaga|ja|足利市}}, Tochigi Prefecture. Some traditions suggest Kagawake is connected to the origin of the place name "Ashikaga." <ref>{{Cite book|title=Japanese Geographic Resources|chapter=Research on Keno Province in the Kofun Period}}</ref>

revision.text:
{{Infobox Ancient Aristocrat | Name = Kagawake | Image = | Image_Size = | Image_Caption = | Era = [[Kofun period]] | Birth_Date = Unknown | Death_Date = Unknown | Alternate_Names = Kagawake no Mikoto, Kannagiwake, Kannagiwake no Mikoto, Kagawake no Ō, Kagawake no Mikoto | Posthumous_Name = | Deity_Name = | Dharma_Name = | Burial_Place = | Titles = [[Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko|First Ukita Kuni no Miyatsuko]] | Allegiances = [[Emperor Seimu]], [[Empress Jingū]], [[Emperor Ōjin]] | Clan = {{ill|Kamitsuke Clan|ja|上毛野氏}}, {{ill|Shimosuke Clan|ja|下毛野氏}}, Ancestor of the {{ill|Kibiya Clan|ja|吉弥侯部}} | Parents = | Spouse = | Children = | Notable_Features = }} '''Kagawake''' (かがわけ{{Sfn|Kagawake (Ancient Clan)|2010}}, birth and death dates unknown) is a historical figure from the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} referenced in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} and other historical texts. He was the first '''{{ill|Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|浮田国造}}''', an ancestor of the {{ill|Kamitsuke Clan|ja|上毛野氏}} and {{ill|Kibiya Clan|ja|吉弥侯部}}. Kagawake is described as a general sent to the Korean Peninsula under {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}'s reign. The "Kannagiwake" (巫別) mentioned during the {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} period is believed to be the same individual{{Sfn|Kagawake (Ancient Clan)|2010}}.
== Records ==
The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} records Kagawake's involvement in the following events: * In the 49th year of {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}, he was appointed a general alongside {{ill|Aratawake|ja|荒田別}} to subjugate {{ill|Silla|ja|新羅}}. His return to Japan is noted in the 50th year. * In the 15th year of {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}, "Kannagiwake" was dispatched with Aratawake to {{ill|Baekje|ja|百済}}. The following year, they returned with {{ill|Wani|ja|王仁}}. "Kannagiwake" is identified with Kagawake{{Sfn|Kagawake (Ancient Clan)|2010}}. This record also describes Aratawake and Kannagiwake as "ancestors of the Kamitsuke Clan." * According to the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}, "Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi" section, "Kagawake no Ō," a fifth-generation descendant of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}, was appointed as the Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko under {{ill|Emperor Seimu|ja|成務天皇}}'s reign.
The name Kagawake also appears in the {{ill|Sumiyoshi Taisha Jindaiki|ja|住吉大社神代記}} in the context of the Silla expedition.
== Analysis ==
Kagawake's existence is debated among scholars. Some argue he was a historical figure based on the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, genealogical records, and references in the {{ill|Baekje Annals|ja|百済記}}. Others suggest he is a later legendary addition{{Sfn|Kagawake (Ancient Clan)|2010}}.
The phonetic similarity of "Kaga" has led to theories linking Kagawake to {{ill|Ashikaga District|ja|足利郡}}, present-day {{ill|Ashikaga|ja|足利市}}, Tochigi Prefecture. Some traditions suggest Kagawake is connected to the origin of the place name "Ashikaga." <ref>{{Cite book|title=Japanese Geographic Resources|chapter=Research on Keno Province in the Kofun Period}}</ref>
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== References == * {{Cite book|title=Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Clans: Popular Edition|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館}}|year=2010|chapter=Kagawake|isbn=978-4642014588|ref={{Harvid|Kagawake (Ancient Clan)|2010}}}}

The "Kannagiwake" (巫別) mentioned during the {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} period is believed to be the same individual{{Sfn|Kagawake (Ancient Clan)|2010}}.
== Records ==
The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} records Kagawake's involvement in the following events: * In the 49th year of {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}, he was appointed a general alongside {{ill|Aratawake|ja|荒田別}} to subjugate {{ill|Silla|ja|新羅}}. His return to Japan is noted in the 50th year. * In the 15th year of {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}, "Kannagiwake" was dispatched with Aratawake to {{ill|Baekje|ja|百済}}. The following year, they returned with {{ill|Wani|ja|王仁}}. "Kannagiwake" is identified with Kagawake{{Sfn|Kagawake (Ancient Clan)|2010}}. This record also describes Aratawake and Kannagiwake as "ancestors of the Kamitsuke Clan." * According to the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}, "Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi" section, "Kagawake no Ō," a fifth-generation descendant of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}, was appointed as the Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko under {{ill|Emperor Seimu|ja|成務天皇}}'s reign.
The name Kagawake also appears in the {{ill|Sumiyoshi Taisha Jindaiki|ja|住吉大社神代記}} in the context of the Silla expedition.
== Analysis ==
Kagawake's existence is debated among scholars. Some argue he was a historical figure based on the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, genealogical records, and references in the {{ill|Baekje Annals|ja|百済記}}. Others suggest he is a later legendary addition{{Sfn|Kagawake (Ancient Clan)|2010}}.
The phonetic similarity of "Kaga" has led to theories linking Kagawake to {{ill|Ashikaga District|ja|足利郡}}, present-day {{ill|Ashikaga|ja|足利市}}, Tochigi Prefecture. Some traditions suggest Kagawake is connected to the origin of the place name "Ashikaga." <ref>{{Cite book|title=Japanese Geographic Resources|chapter=Research on Keno Province in the Kofun Period}}</ref>

The name Kagawake also appears in the {{ill|Sumiyoshi Taisha Jindaiki|ja|住吉大社神代記}} in the context of the Silla expedition.
== Analysis ==
Kagawake's existence is debated among scholars. Some argue he was a historical figure based on the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, genealogical records, and references in the {{ill|Baekje Annals|ja|百済記}}. Others suggest he is a later legendary addition{{Sfn|Kagawake (Ancient Clan)|2010}}.
The phonetic similarity of "Kaga" has led to theories linking Kagawake to {{ill|Ashikaga District|ja|足利郡}}, present-day {{ill|Ashikaga|ja|足利市}}, Tochigi Prefecture. Some traditions suggest Kagawake is connected to the origin of the place name "Ashikaga." <ref>{{Cite book|title=Japanese Geographic Resources|chapter=Research on Keno Province in the Kofun Period}}</ref>

== References == * {{Cite book|title=Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Clans: Popular Edition|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館}}|year=2010|chapter=Kagawake|isbn=978-4642014588|ref={{Harvid|Kagawake (Ancient Clan)|2010}}}}

{{Infobox Ancient Aristocrat | Name = Kagawake | Image = | Image_Size = | Image_Caption = | Era = [[Kofun period]] | Birth_Date = Unknown | Death_Date = Unknown | Alternate_Names = Kagawake no Mikoto, Kannagiwake, Kannagiwake no Mikoto, Kagawake no Ō, Kagawake no Mikoto | Posthumous_Name = | Deity_Name = | Dharma_Name = | Burial_Place = | Titles = [[Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko|First Ukita Kuni no Miyatsuko]] | Allegiances = [[Emperor Seimu]], [[Empress Jingū]], [[Emperor Ōjin]] | Clan = {{ill|Kamitsuke Clan|ja|上毛野氏}}, {{ill|Shimosuke Clan|ja|下毛野氏}}, Ancestor of the {{ill|Kibiya Clan|ja|吉弥侯部}} | Parents = | Spouse = | Children = | Notable_Features = }} '''Kagawake''' (かがわけ{{Sfn|Kagawake (Ancient Clan)|2010}}, birth and death dates unknown) is a historical figure from the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} referenced in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} and other historical texts. Kagawake is described as a general sent to the Korean Peninsula under {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}'s reign. The "Kannagiwake" (巫別) mentioned during the {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} period is believed to be the same individual{{Sfn|Kagawake (Ancient Clan)|2010}}.

The phonetic similarity of "Kaga" has led to theories linking Kagawake to {{ill|Ashikaga District|ja|足利郡}}, present-day {{ill|Ashikaga|ja|足利市}}, Tochigi Prefecture. Some traditions suggest Kagawake is connected to the origin of the place name "Ashikaga." <ref>{{Cite book|title=Japanese Geographic Resources|chapter=Research on Keno Province in the Kofun Period}}</ref>

revision.comment: forking the category so massreplace can work better on it, fixing category typo
revision.text:
{{Infobox Ancient Aristocrat | Name = Kagawake | Image = | Image_Size = | Image_Caption = | Era = [[Kofun period]] | Birth_Date = Unknown | Death_Date = Unknown | Alternate_Names = Kagawake no Mikoto, Kannagiwake, Kannagiwake no Mikoto, Kagawake no Ō, Kagawake no Mikoto | Posthumous_Name = | Deity_Name = | Dharma_Name = | Burial_Place = | Titles = [[Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko|First Ukita Kuni no Miyatsuko]] | Allegiances = [[Emperor Seimu]], [[Empress Jingū]], [[Emperor Ōjin]] | Clan = {{ill|Kamitsuke Clan|ja|上毛野氏}}, {{ill|Shimosuke Clan|ja|下毛野氏}}, Ancestor of the {{ill|Kibiya Clan|ja|吉弥侯部}} | Parents = | Spouse = | Children = | Notable_Features = }} '''Kagawake''' (かがわけ{{Sfn|Kagawake (Ancient Clan)|2010}}, birth and death dates unknown) is a historical figure from the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} referenced in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} and other historical texts. He was the first '''{{ill|Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|浮田国造}}''', an ancestor of the {{ill|Kamitsuke Clan|ja|上毛野氏}} and {{ill|Kibiya Clan|ja|吉弥侯部}}. Kagawake is described as a general sent to the Korean Peninsula under {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}'s reign. The "Kannagiwake" (巫別) mentioned during the {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} period is believed to be the same individual{{Sfn|Kagawake (Ancient Clan)|2010}}.
== Records ==
The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} records Kagawake's involvement in the following events: * In the 49th year of {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}, he was appointed a general alongside {{ill|Aratawake|ja|荒田別}} to subjugate {{ill|Silla|ja|新羅}}. His return to Japan is noted in the 50th year. * In the 15th year of {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}, "Kannagiwake" was dispatched with Aratawake to {{ill|Baekje|ja|百済}}. The following year, they returned with {{ill|Wani|ja|王仁}}. "Kannagiwake" is identified with Kagawake{{Sfn|Kagawake (Ancient Clan)|2010}}. This record also describes Aratawake and Kannagiwake as "ancestors of the Kamitsuke Clan." * According to the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}, "Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi" section, "Kagawake no Ō," a fifth-generation descendant of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}, was appointed as the Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko under {{ill|Emperor Seimu|ja|成務天皇}}'s reign.
The name Kagawake also appears in the {{ill|Sumiyoshi Taisha Jindaiki|ja|住吉大社神代記}} in the context of the Silla expedition.
== Analysis ==
Kagawake's existence is debated among scholars. Some argue he was a historical figure based on the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, genealogical records, and references in the {{ill|Baekje Annals|ja|百済記}}. Others suggest he is a later legendary addition{{Sfn|Kagawake (Ancient Clan)|2010}}.
The phonetic similarity of "Kaga" has led to theories linking Kagawake to {{ill|Ashikaga District|ja|足利郡}}, present-day {{ill|Ashikaga|ja|足利市}}, Tochigi Prefecture. Some traditions suggest Kagawake is connected to the origin of the place name "Ashikaga." <ref>{{Cite book|title=Japanese Geographic Resources|chapter=Research on Keno Province in the Kofun Period}}</ref>
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== References == * {{Cite book|title=Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Clans: Popular Edition|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館}}|year=2010|chapter=Kagawake|isbn=978-4642014588|ref={{Harvid|Kagawake (Ancient Clan)|2010}}}}
== Related Topics == * {{ill|List of pre-Asuka period figures|ja|飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧}} * {{ill|Kamitsuke Clan|ja|上毛野氏}}

revision.comment: Adding [[Category:kk]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{Infobox Ancient Aristocrat | Name = Kagawake | Image = | Image_Size = | Image_Caption = | Era = [[Kofun period]] | Birth_Date = Unknown | Death_Date = Unknown | Alternate_Names = Kagawake no Mikoto, Kannagiwake, Kannagiwake no Mikoto, Kagawake no Ō, Kagawake no Mikoto | Posthumous_Name = | Deity_Name = | Dharma_Name = | Burial_Place = | Titles = [[Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko|First Ukita Kuni no Miyatsuko]] | Allegiances = [[Emperor Seimu]], [[Empress Jingū]], [[Emperor Ōjin]] | Clan = {{ill|Kamitsuke Clan|ja|上毛野氏}}, {{ill|Shimosuke Clan|ja|下毛野氏}}, Ancestor of the {{ill|Kibiya Clan|ja|吉弥侯部}} | Parents = | Spouse = | Children = | Notable_Features = }} '''Kagawake''' (かがわけ{{Sfn|Kagawake (Ancient Clan)|2010}}, birth and death dates unknown) is a historical figure from the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} referenced in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} and other historical texts. He was the first '''{{ill|Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|浮田国造}}''', an ancestor of the {{ill|Kamitsuke Clan|ja|上毛野氏}} and {{ill|Kibiya Clan|ja|吉弥侯部}}. Kagawake is described as a general sent to the Korean Peninsula under {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}'s reign. The "Kannagiwake" (巫別) mentioned during the {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} period is believed to be the same individual{{Sfn|Kagawake (Ancient Clan)|2010}}.
== Records ==
The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} records Kagawake's involvement in the following events: * In the 49th year of {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}, he was appointed a general alongside {{ill|Aratawake|ja|荒田別}} to subjugate {{ill|Silla|ja|新羅}}. His return to Japan is noted in the 50th year. * In the 15th year of {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}, "Kannagiwake" was dispatched with Aratawake to {{ill|Baekje|ja|百済}}. The following year, they returned with {{ill|Wani|ja|王仁}}. "Kannagiwake" is identified with Kagawake{{Sfn|Kagawake (Ancient Clan)|2010}}. This record also describes Aratawake and Kannagiwake as "ancestors of the Kamitsuke Clan." * According to the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}, "Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi" section, "Kagawake no Ō," a fifth-generation descendant of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}, was appointed as the Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko under {{ill|Emperor Seimu|ja|成務天皇}}'s reign.
The name Kagawake also appears in the {{ill|Sumiyoshi Taisha Jindaiki|ja|住吉大社神代記}} in the context of the Silla expedition.
== Analysis ==
Kagawake's existence is debated among scholars. Some argue he was a historical figure based on the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, genealogical records, and references in the {{ill|Baekje Annals|ja|百済記}}. Others suggest he is a later legendary addition{{Sfn|Kagawake (Ancient Clan)|2010}}.
The phonetic similarity of "Kaga" has led to theories linking Kagawake to {{ill|Ashikaga District|ja|足利郡}}, present-day {{ill|Ashikaga|ja|足利市}}, Tochigi Prefecture. Some traditions suggest Kagawake is connected to the origin of the place name "Ashikaga." <ref>{{Cite book|title=Japanese Geographic Resources|chapter=Research on Keno Province in the Kofun Period}}</ref>
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== References == * {{Cite book|title=Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Clans: Popular Edition|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館}}|year=2010|chapter=Kagawake|isbn=978-4642014588|ref={{Harvid|Kagawake (Ancient Clan)|2010}}}}
== Related Topics == * {{ill|List of pre-Asuka period figures|ja|飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧}} * {{ill|Kamitsuke Clan|ja|上毛野氏}}

This record also describes Aratawake and Kannagiwake as "ancestors of the Kamitsuke Clan." * According to the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}, "Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi" section, "Kagawake no Ō," a fifth-generation descendant of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}, was appointed as the Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko under {{ill|Emperor Seimu|ja|成務天皇}}'s reign.
The name Kagawake also appears in the {{ill|Sumiyoshi Taisha Jindaiki|ja|住吉大社神代記}} in the context of the Silla expedition.
== Analysis ==
Kagawake's existence is debated among scholars. Some argue he was a historical figure based on the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, genealogical records, and references in the {{ill|Baekje Annals|ja|百済記}}. Others suggest he is a later legendary addition{{Sfn|Kagawake (Ancient Clan)|2010}}.
The phonetic similarity of "Kaga" has led to theories linking Kagawake to {{ill|Ashikaga District|ja|足利郡}}, present-day {{ill|Ashikaga|ja|足利市}}, Tochigi Prefecture. Some traditions suggest Kagawake is connected to the origin of the place name "Ashikaga." <ref>{{Cite book|title=Japanese Geographic Resources|chapter=Research on Keno Province in the Kofun Period}}</ref>

{{Infobox Ancient Aristocrat | Name = Kagawake | Image = | Image_Size = | Image_Caption = | Era = [[Kofun period]] | Birth_Date = Unknown | Death_Date = Unknown | Alternate_Names = Kagawake no Mikoto, Kannagiwake, Kannagiwake no Mikoto, Kagawake no Ō, Kagawake no Mikoto | Posthumous_Name = | Deity_Name = | Dharma_Name = | Burial_Place = | Titles = [[Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko|First Ukita Kuni no Miyatsuko]] | Allegiances = [[Emperor Seimu]], [[Empress Jingū]], [[Emperor Ōjin]] | Clan = {{ill|Kamitsuke Clan|ja|上毛野氏}}, {{ill|Shimosuke Clan|ja|下毛野氏}}, Ancestor of the {{ill|Kibiya Clan|ja|吉弥侯部}} | Parents = | Spouse = | Children = | Notable_Features = }} '''Kagawake''' (かがわけ{{Sfn|Kagawake (Ancient Clan)|2010}}, birth and death dates unknown) is a historical figure from the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} referenced in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} and other historical texts. He was the first '''{{ill|Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|浮田国造}}''', an ancestor of the {{ill|Kamitsuke Clan|ja|上毛野氏}} and {{ill|Kibiya Clan|ja|吉弥侯部}}. Kagawake is described as a general sent to the Korean Peninsula under {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}'s reign. The "Kannagiwake" (巫別) mentioned during the {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} period is believed to be the same individual{{Sfn|Kagawake (Ancient Clan)|2010}}.
== Records ==
The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} records Kagawake's involvement in the following events: * In the 49th year of {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}, he was appointed a general alongside {{ill|Aratawake|ja|荒田別}} to subjugate {{ill|Silla|ja|新羅}}. His return to Japan is noted in the 50th year. * In the 15th year of {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}, "Kannagiwake" was dispatched with Aratawake to {{ill|Baekje|ja|百済}}. The following year, they returned with {{ill|Wani|ja|王仁}}. "Kannagiwake" is identified with Kagawake{{Sfn|Kagawake (Ancient Clan)|2010}}. This record also describes Aratawake and Kannagiwake as "ancestors of the Kamitsuke Clan." * According to the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}, "Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi" section, "Kagawake no Ō," a fifth-generation descendant of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}, was appointed as the Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko under {{ill|Emperor Seimu|ja|成務天皇}}'s reign.
The name Kagawake also appears in the {{ill|Sumiyoshi Taisha Jindaiki|ja|住吉大社神代記}} in the context of the Silla expedition.
== Analysis ==
Kagawake's existence is debated among scholars. Some argue he was a historical figure based on the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, genealogical records, and references in the {{ill|Baekje Annals|ja|百済記}}. Others suggest he is a later legendary addition{{Sfn|Kagawake (Ancient Clan)|2010}}.
The phonetic similarity of "Kaga" has led to theories linking Kagawake to {{ill|Ashikaga District|ja|足利郡}}, present-day {{ill|Ashikaga|ja|足利市}}, Tochigi Prefecture. Some traditions suggest Kagawake is connected to the origin of the place name "Ashikaga." <ref>{{Cite book|title=Japanese Geographic Resources|chapter=Research on Keno Province in the Kofun Period}}</ref>
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== References == * {{Cite book|title=Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Clans: Popular Edition|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館}}|year=2010|chapter=Kagawake|isbn=978-4642014588|ref={{Harvid|Kagawake (Ancient Clan)|2010}}}}

revision.comment: Adding [[Category:subst:MAINSPACE]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{Infobox Ancient Aristocrat | Name = Kagawake | Image = | Image_Size = | Image_Caption = | Era = [[Kofun period]] | Birth_Date = Unknown | Death_Date = Unknown | Alternate_Names = Kagawake no Mikoto, Kannagiwake, Kannagiwake no Mikoto, Kagawake no Ō, Kagawake no Mikoto | Posthumous_Name = | Deity_Name = | Dharma_Name = | Burial_Place = | Titles = [[Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko|First Ukita Kuni no Miyatsuko]] | Allegiances = [[Emperor Seimu]], [[Empress Jingū]], [[Emperor Ōjin]] | Clan = {{ill|Kamitsuke Clan|ja|上毛野氏}}, {{ill|Shimosuke Clan|ja|下毛野氏}}, Ancestor of the {{ill|Kibiya Clan|ja|吉弥侯部}} | Parents = | Spouse = | Children = | Notable_Features = }} '''Kagawake''' (かがわけ{{Sfn|Kagawake (Ancient Clan)|2010}}, birth and death dates unknown) is a historical figure from the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} referenced in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} and other historical texts. He was the first '''{{ill|Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|浮田国造}}''', an ancestor of the {{ill|Kamitsuke Clan|ja|上毛野氏}} and {{ill|Kibiya Clan|ja|吉弥侯部}}. Kagawake is described as a general sent to the Korean Peninsula under {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}'s reign. The "Kannagiwake" (巫別) mentioned during the {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} period is believed to be the same individual{{Sfn|Kagawake (Ancient Clan)|2010}}.
== Records ==
The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} records Kagawake's involvement in the following events: * In the 49th year of {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}, he was appointed a general alongside {{ill|Aratawake|ja|荒田別}} to subjugate {{ill|Silla|ja|新羅}}. His return to Japan is noted in the 50th year. * In the 15th year of {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}, "Kannagiwake" was dispatched with Aratawake to {{ill|Baekje|ja|百済}}. The following year, they returned with {{ill|Wani|ja|王仁}}. "Kannagiwake" is identified with Kagawake{{Sfn|Kagawake (Ancient Clan)|2010}}. This record also describes Aratawake and Kannagiwake as "ancestors of the Kamitsuke Clan." * According to the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}, "Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi" section, "Kagawake no Ō," a fifth-generation descendant of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}, was appointed as the Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko under {{ill|Emperor Seimu|ja|成務天皇}}'s reign.
The name Kagawake also appears in the {{ill|Sumiyoshi Taisha Jindaiki|ja|住吉大社神代記}} in the context of the Silla expedition.
== Analysis ==
Kagawake's existence is debated among scholars. Some argue he was a historical figure based on the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, genealogical records, and references in the {{ill|Baekje Annals|ja|百済記}}. Others suggest he is a later legendary addition{{Sfn|Kagawake (Ancient Clan)|2010}}.
The phonetic similarity of "Kaga" has led to theories linking Kagawake to {{ill|Ashikaga District|ja|足利郡}}, present-day {{ill|Ashikaga|ja|足利市}}, Tochigi Prefecture. Some traditions suggest Kagawake is connected to the origin of the place name "Ashikaga." <ref>{{Cite book|title=Japanese Geographic Resources|chapter=Research on Keno Province in the Kofun Period}}</ref>
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== References == * {{Cite book|title=Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Clans: Popular Edition|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館}}|year=2010|chapter=Kagawake|isbn=978-4642014588|ref={{Harvid|Kagawake (Ancient Clan)|2010}}}}
== Related Topics == * {{ill|List of pre-Asuka period figures|ja|飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧}} * {{ill|Kamitsuke Clan|ja|上毛野氏}}

{{Infobox Ancient Aristocrat | Name = Kagawake | Image = | Image_Size = | Image_Caption = | Era = [[Kofun period]] | Birth_Date = Unknown | Death_Date = Unknown | Alternate_Names = Kagawake no Mikoto, Kannagiwake, Kannagiwake no Mikoto, Kagawake no Ō, Kagawake no Mikoto | Posthumous_Name = | Deity_Name = | Dharma_Name = | Burial_Place = | Titles = [[Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko|First Ukita Kuni no Miyatsuko]] | Allegiances = [[Emperor Seimu]], [[Empress Jingū]], [[Emperor Ōjin]] | Clan = {{ill|Kamitsuke Clan|ja|上毛野氏}}, {{ill|Shimosuke Clan|ja|下毛野氏}}, Ancestor of the {{ill|Kibiya Clan|ja|吉弥侯部}} | Parents = | Spouse = | Children = | Notable_Features = }} '''Kagawake''' (かがわけ{{Sfn|Kagawake (Ancient Clan)|2010}}, birth and death dates unknown) is a historical figure from the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} referenced in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} and other historical texts. He was the first '''{{ill|Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|浮田国造}}''', an ancestor of the {{ill|Kamitsuke Clan|ja|上毛野氏}} and {{ill|Kibiya Clan|ja|吉弥侯部}}. Kagawake is described as a general sent to the Korean Peninsula under {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}'s reign. The "Kannagiwake" (巫別) mentioned during the {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} period is believed to be the same individual{{Sfn|Kagawake (Ancient Clan)|2010}}.
== Records ==
The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} records Kagawake's involvement in the following events: * In the 49th year of {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}, he was appointed a general alongside {{ill|Aratawake|ja|荒田別}} to subjugate {{ill|Silla|ja|新羅}}. His return to Japan is noted in the 50th year. * In the 15th year of {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}, "Kannagiwake" was dispatched with Aratawake to {{ill|Baekje|ja|百済}}. The following year, they returned with {{ill|Wani|ja|王仁}}. "Kannagiwake" is identified with Kagawake{{Sfn|Kagawake (Ancient Clan)|2010}}. This record also describes Aratawake and Kannagiwake as "ancestors of the Kamitsuke Clan." * According to the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}, "Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi" section, "Kagawake no Ō," a fifth-generation descendant of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}, was appointed as the Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko under {{ill|Emperor Seimu|ja|成務天皇}}'s reign.
The name Kagawake also appears in the {{ill|Sumiyoshi Taisha Jindaiki|ja|住吉大社神代記}} in the context of the Silla expedition.
== Analysis ==
Kagawake's existence is debated among scholars. Some argue he was a historical figure based on the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, genealogical records, and references in the {{ill|Baekje Annals|ja|百済記}}. Others suggest he is a later legendary addition{{Sfn|Kagawake (Ancient Clan)|2010}}.
The phonetic similarity of "Kaga" has led to theories linking Kagawake to {{ill|Ashikaga District|ja|足利郡}}, present-day {{ill|Ashikaga|ja|足利市}}, Tochigi Prefecture. Some traditions suggest Kagawake is connected to the origin of the place name "Ashikaga." <ref>{{Cite book|title=Japanese Geographic Resources|chapter=Research on Keno Province in the Kofun Period}}</ref>
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== References == * {{Cite book|title=Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Clans: Popular Edition|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館}}|year=2010|chapter=Kagawake|isbn=978-4642014588|ref={{Harvid|Kagawake (Ancient Clan)|2010}}}}
== Related Topics == * {{ill|List of pre-Asuka period figures|ja|飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧}} * {{ill|Kamitsuke Clan|ja|上毛野氏}}

The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} records Kagawake's involvement in the following events: * In the 49th year of {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}, he was appointed a general alongside {{ill|Aratawake|ja|荒田別}} to subjugate {{ill|Silla|ja|新羅}}. His return to Japan is noted in the 50th year. * In the 15th year of {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}, "Kannagiwake" was dispatched with Aratawake to {{ill|Baekje|ja|百済}}. The following year, they returned with {{ill|Wani|ja|王仁}}. "Kannagiwake" is identified with Kagawake{{Sfn|Kagawake (Ancient Clan)|2010}}. This record also describes Aratawake and Kannagiwake as "ancestors of the Kamitsuke Clan." * According to the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}, "Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi" section, "Kagawake no Ō," a fifth-generation descendant of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}, was appointed as the Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko under {{ill|Emperor Seimu|ja|成務天皇}}'s reign.
The name Kagawake also appears in the {{ill|Sumiyoshi Taisha Jindaiki|ja|住吉大社神代記}} in the context of the Silla expedition.
== Analysis ==
Kagawake's existence is debated among scholars. Some argue he was a historical figure based on the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, genealogical records, and references in the {{ill|Baekje Annals|ja|百済記}}. Others suggest he is a later legendary addition{{Sfn|Kagawake (Ancient Clan)|2010}}.
The phonetic similarity of "Kaga" has led to theories linking Kagawake to {{ill|Ashikaga District|ja|足利郡}}, present-day {{ill|Ashikaga|ja|足利市}}, Tochigi Prefecture. Some traditions suggest Kagawake is connected to the origin of the place name "Ashikaga." <ref>{{Cite book|title=Japanese Geographic Resources|chapter=Research on Keno Province in the Kofun Period}}</ref>
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== References == * {{Cite book|title=Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Clans: Popular Edition|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館}}|year=2010|chapter=Kagawake|isbn=978-4642014588|ref={{Harvid|Kagawake (Ancient Clan)|2010}}}}
== Related Topics == * {{ill|List of pre-Asuka period figures|ja|飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧}} * {{ill|Kamitsuke Clan|ja|上毛野氏}}

{{Infobox Ancient Aristocrat | Name = Kagawake | Image = | Image_Size = | Image_Caption = | Era = [[Kofun period]] | Birth_Date = Unknown | Death_Date = Unknown | Alternate_Names = Kagawake no Mikoto, Kannagiwake, Kannagiwake no Mikoto, Kagawake no Ō, Kagawake no Mikoto | Posthumous_Name = | Deity_Name = | Dharma_Name = | Burial_Place = | Titles = [[Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko|First Ukita Kuni no Miyatsuko]] | Allegiances = [[Emperor Seimu]], [[Empress Jingū]], [[Emperor Ōjin]] | Clan = {{ill|Kamitsuke Clan|ja|上毛野氏}}, {{ill|Shimosuke Clan|ja|下毛野氏}}, Ancestor of the {{ill|Kibiya Clan|ja|吉弥侯部}} | Parents = | Spouse = | Children = | Notable_Features = }} '''Kagawake''' (かがわけ{{Sfn|Kagawake (Ancient Clan)|2010}}, birth and death dates unknown) is a historical figure from the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} referenced in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} and other historical texts. He was the first '''{{ill|Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|浮田国造}}''', an ancestor of the {{ill|Kamitsuke Clan|ja|上毛野氏}} and {{ill|Kibiya Clan|ja|吉弥侯部}}. Kagawake is described as a general sent to the Korean Peninsula under {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}'s reign. The "Kannagiwake" (巫別) mentioned during the {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} period is believed to be the same individual{{Sfn|Kagawake (Ancient Clan)|2010}}.
== Records ==
The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} records Kagawake's involvement in the following events: * In the 49th year of {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}, he was appointed a general alongside {{ill|Aratawake|ja|荒田別}} to subjugate {{ill|Silla|ja|新羅}}. His return to Japan is noted in the 50th year. * In the 15th year of {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}, "Kannagiwake" was dispatched with Aratawake to {{ill|Baekje|ja|百済}}. The following year, they returned with {{ill|Wani|ja|王仁}}. "Kannagiwake" is identified with Kagawake{{Sfn|Kagawake (Ancient Clan)|2010}}. This record also describes Aratawake and Kannagiwake as "ancestors of the Kamitsuke Clan." * According to the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}, "Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi" section, "Kagawake no Ō," a fifth-generation descendant of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}, was appointed as the Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko under {{ill|Emperor Seimu|ja|成務天皇}}'s reign.
The name Kagawake also appears in the {{ill|Sumiyoshi Taisha Jindaiki|ja|住吉大社神代記}} in the context of the Silla expedition.
== Analysis ==
Kagawake's existence is debated among scholars. Some argue he was a historical figure based on the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, genealogical records, and references in the {{ill|Baekje Annals|ja|百済記}}. Others suggest he is a later legendary addition{{Sfn|Kagawake (Ancient Clan)|2010}}.
The phonetic similarity of "Kaga" has led to theories linking Kagawake to {{ill|Ashikaga District|ja|足利郡}}, present-day {{ill|Ashikaga|ja|足利市}}, Tochigi Prefecture. Some traditions suggest Kagawake is connected to the origin of the place name "Ashikaga." <ref>{{Cite book|title=Japanese Geographic Resources|chapter=Research on Keno Province in the Kofun Period}}</ref>
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== References == * {{Cite book|title=Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Clans: Popular Edition|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館}}|year=2010|chapter=Kagawake|isbn=978-4642014588|ref={{Harvid|Kagawake (Ancient Clan)|2010}}}}

revision.comment: Removing this excess
revision.text:
{{Infobox Ancient Aristocrat | Name = Kagawake | Image = | Image_Size = | Image_Caption = | Era = [[Kofun period]] | Birth_Date = Unknown | Death_Date = Unknown | Alternate_Names = Kagawake no Mikoto, Kannagiwake, Kannagiwake no Mikoto, Kagawake no Ō, Kagawake no Mikoto | Posthumous_Name = | Deity_Name = | Dharma_Name = | Burial_Place = | Titles = [[Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko|First Ukita Kuni no Miyatsuko]] | Allegiances = [[Emperor Seimu]], [[Empress Jingū]], [[Emperor Ōjin]] | Clan = {{ill|Kamitsuke Clan|ja|上毛野氏}}, {{ill|Shimosuke Clan|ja|下毛野氏}}, Ancestor of the {{ill|Kibiya Clan|ja|吉弥侯部}} | Parents = | Spouse = | Children = | Notable_Features = }} '''Kagawake''' (かがわけ{{Sfn|Kagawake (Ancient Clan)|2010}}, birth and death dates unknown) is a historical figure from the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} referenced in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} and other historical texts. He was the first '''{{ill|Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|浮田国造}}''', an ancestor of the {{ill|Kamitsuke Clan|ja|上毛野氏}} and {{ill|Kibiya Clan|ja|吉弥侯部}}. Kagawake is described as a general sent to the Korean Peninsula under {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}'s reign. The "Kannagiwake" (巫別) mentioned during the {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} period is believed to be the same individual{{Sfn|Kagawake (Ancient Clan)|2010}}.
== Records ==
The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} records Kagawake's involvement in the following events: * In the 49th year of {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}, he was appointed a general alongside {{ill|Aratawake|ja|荒田別}} to subjugate {{ill|Silla|ja|新羅}}. His return to Japan is noted in the 50th year. * In the 15th year of {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}, "Kannagiwake" was dispatched with Aratawake to {{ill|Baekje|ja|百済}}. The following year, they returned with {{ill|Wani|ja|王仁}}. "Kannagiwake" is identified with Kagawake{{Sfn|Kagawake (Ancient Clan)|2010}}. This record also describes Aratawake and Kannagiwake as "ancestors of the Kamitsuke Clan." * According to the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}, "Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi" section, "Kagawake no Ō," a fifth-generation descendant of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}, was appointed as the Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko under {{ill|Emperor Seimu|ja|成務天皇}}'s reign.
The name Kagawake also appears in the {{ill|Sumiyoshi Taisha Jindaiki|ja|住吉大社神代記}} in the context of the Silla expedition.
== Analysis ==
Kagawake's existence is debated among scholars. Some argue he was a historical figure based on the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, genealogical records, and references in the {{ill|Baekje Annals|ja|百済記}}. Others suggest he is a later legendary addition{{Sfn|Kagawake (Ancient Clan)|2010}}.
The phonetic similarity of "Kaga" has led to theories linking Kagawake to {{ill|Ashikaga District|ja|足利郡}}, present-day {{ill|Ashikaga|ja|足利市}}, Tochigi Prefecture. Some traditions suggest Kagawake is connected to the origin of the place name "Ashikaga." <ref>{{Cite book|title=Japanese Geographic Resources|chapter=Research on Keno Province in the Kofun Period}}</ref>
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== References == * {{Cite book|title=Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Clans: Popular Edition|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館}}|year=2010|chapter=Kagawake|isbn=978-4642014588|ref={{Harvid|Kagawake (Ancient Clan)|2010}}}}
== Related Topics == * {{ill|List of pre-Asuka period figures|ja|飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧}} * {{ill|Kamitsuke Clan|ja|上毛野氏}}

revision.comment: Text replacement - "下毛野氏}}" to "下毛野氏|lt=Shimotsukeno clan}}"
revision.text:
{{Infobox Ancient Aristocrat | Name = Kagawake | Image = | Image_Size = | Image_Caption = | Era = [[Kofun period]] | Birth_Date = Unknown | Death_Date = Unknown | Alternate_Names = Kagawake no Mikoto, Kannagiwake, Kannagiwake no Mikoto, Kagawake no Ō, Kagawake no Mikoto | Posthumous_Name = | Deity_Name = | Dharma_Name = | Burial_Place = | Titles = [[Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko|First Ukita Kuni no Miyatsuko]] | Allegiances = [[Emperor Seimu]], [[Empress Jingū]], [[Emperor Ōjin]] | Clan = {{ill|Kamitsuke Clan|ja|上毛野氏}}, {{ill|Shimosuke Clan|ja|下毛野氏|lt=Shimotsukeno clan}}, Ancestor of the {{ill|Kibiya Clan|ja|吉弥侯部}} | Parents = | Spouse = | Children = | Notable_Features = }} '''Kagawake''' (かがわけ{{Sfn|Kagawake (Ancient Clan)|2010}}, birth and death dates unknown) is a historical figure from the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} referenced in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} and other historical texts. He was the first '''{{ill|Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|浮田国造}}''', an ancestor of the {{ill|Kamitsuke Clan|ja|上毛野氏}} and {{ill|Kibiya Clan|ja|吉弥侯部}}. Kagawake is described as a general sent to the Korean Peninsula under {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}'s reign. The "Kannagiwake" (巫別) mentioned during the {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} period is believed to be the same individual{{Sfn|Kagawake (Ancient Clan)|2010}}.
== Records ==
The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} records Kagawake's involvement in the following events: * In the 49th year of {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}, he was appointed a general alongside {{ill|Aratawake|ja|荒田別}} to subjugate {{ill|Silla|ja|新羅}}. His return to Japan is noted in the 50th year. * In the 15th year of {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}, "Kannagiwake" was dispatched with Aratawake to {{ill|Baekje|ja|百済}}. The following year, they returned with {{ill|Wani|ja|王仁}}. "Kannagiwake" is identified with Kagawake{{Sfn|Kagawake (Ancient Clan)|2010}}. This record also describes Aratawake and Kannagiwake as "ancestors of the Kamitsuke Clan." * According to the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}, "Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi" section, "Kagawake no Ō," a fifth-generation descendant of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}, was appointed as the Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko under {{ill|Emperor Seimu|ja|成務天皇}}'s reign.
The name Kagawake also appears in the {{ill|Sumiyoshi Taisha Jindaiki|ja|住吉大社神代記}} in the context of the Silla expedition.
== Analysis ==
Kagawake's existence is debated among scholars. Some argue he was a historical figure based on the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, genealogical records, and references in the {{ill|Baekje Annals|ja|百済記}}. Others suggest he is a later legendary addition{{Sfn|Kagawake (Ancient Clan)|2010}}.
The phonetic similarity of "Kaga" has led to theories linking Kagawake to {{ill|Ashikaga District|ja|足利郡}}, present-day {{ill|Ashikaga|ja|足利市}}, Tochigi Prefecture. Some traditions suggest Kagawake is connected to the origin of the place name "Ashikaga." <ref>{{Cite book|title=Japanese Geographic Resources|chapter=Research on Keno Province in the Kofun Period}}</ref>
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== References == * {{Cite book|title=Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Clans: Popular Edition|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館}}|year=2010|chapter=Kagawake|isbn=978-4642014588|ref={{Harvid|Kagawake (Ancient Clan)|2010}}}}
== See Also == * {{ill|List of pre-Asuka period figures|ja|飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧}} * {{ill|Kamitsuke Clan|ja|上毛野氏}}

{{Infobox Ancient Aristocrat | Name = Kagawake | Image = | Image_Size = | Image_Caption = | Era = [[Kofun period]] | Birth_Date = Unknown | Death_Date = Unknown | Alternate_Names = Kagawake no Mikoto, Kannagiwake, Kannagiwake no Mikoto, Kagawake no Ō, Kagawake no Mikoto | Posthumous_Name = | Deity_Name = | Dharma_Name = | Burial_Place = | Titles = [[Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko|First Ukita Kuni no Miyatsuko]] | Allegiances = [[Emperor Seimu]], [[Empress Jingū]], [[Emperor Ōjin]] | Clan = {{ill|Kamitsuke Clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan}}, {{ill|Shimosuke Clan|ja|下毛野氏|lt=Shimotsukeno clan}}, Ancestor of the {{ill|Kibiya Clan|ja|吉弥侯部}} | Parents = | Spouse = | Children = | Notable_Features = }} '''Kagawake''' (かがわけ{{Sfn|Kagawake (Ancient Clan)|2010}}, birth and death dates unknown) is a historical figure from the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} referenced in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} and other historical texts. He was the first '''{{ill|Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|浮田国造}}''', an ancestor of the {{ill|Kamitsuke Clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan}} and {{ill|Kibiya Clan|ja|吉弥侯部}}. Kagawake is described as a general sent to the Korean Peninsula under {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}'s reign. The "Kannagiwake" (巫別) mentioned during the {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} period is believed to be the same individual{{Sfn|Kagawake (Ancient Clan)|2010}}.
== Records ==
The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} records Kagawake's involvement in the following events: * In the 49th year of {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}, he was appointed a general alongside {{ill|Aratawake|ja|荒田別|1=Aratawake}} to subjugate {{ill|Silla|ja|新羅}}. His return to Japan is noted in the 50th year. * In the 15th year of {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}, "Kannagiwake" was dispatched with Aratawake to {{ill|Baekje|ja|百済}}. The following year, they returned with {{ill|Wani|ja|王仁}}. "Kannagiwake" is identified with Kagawake{{Sfn|Kagawake (Ancient Clan)|2010}}. This record also describes Aratawake and Kannagiwake as "ancestors of the Kamitsuke Clan." * According to the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}, "Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi" section, "Kagawake no Ō," a fifth-generation descendant of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}, was appointed as the Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko under {{ill|Emperor Seimu|ja|成務天皇}}'s reign.
The name Kagawake also appears in the {{ill|Sumiyoshi Taisha Jindaiki|ja|住吉大社神代記}} in the context of the Silla expedition.
== Analysis ==
Kagawake's existence is debated among scholars. Some argue he was a historical figure based on the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, genealogical records, and references in the {{ill|Baekje Annals|ja|百済記}}. Others suggest he is a later legendary addition{{Sfn|Kagawake (Ancient Clan)|2010}}.
The phonetic similarity of "Kaga" has led to theories linking Kagawake to {{ill|Ashikaga District|ja|足利郡}}, present-day {{ill|Ashikaga|ja|足利市}}, Tochigi Prefecture. Some traditions suggest Kagawake is connected to the origin of the place name "Ashikaga." <ref>{{Cite book|title=Japanese Geographic Resources|chapter=Research on Keno Province in the Kofun Period}}</ref>
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== References == * {{Cite book|title=Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Clans: Popular Edition|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館}}|year=2010|chapter=Kagawake|isbn=978-4642014588|ref={{Harvid|Kagawake (Ancient Clan)|2010}}}}
== See Also == * {{ill|List of pre-Asuka period figures|ja|飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧}} * {{ill|Kamitsuke Clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan}}

His return to Japan is noted in the 50th year. * In the 15th year of {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}, "Kannagiwake" was dispatched with Aratawake to {{ill|Baekje|ja|百済}}. The following year, they returned with {{ill|Wani|ja|王仁}}. "Kannagiwake" is identified with Kagawake{{Sfn|Kagawake (Ancient Clan)|2010}}. This record also describes Aratawake and Kannagiwake as "ancestors of the Kamitsuke Clan." * According to the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}, "Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi" section, "Kagawake no Ō," a fifth-generation descendant of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}, was appointed as the Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko under {{ill|Emperor Seimu|ja|成務天皇}}'s reign.
The name Kagawake also appears in the {{ill|Sumiyoshi Taisha Jindaiki|ja|住吉大社神代記}} in the context of the Silla expedition.
== Analysis ==
Kagawake's existence is debated among scholars. Some argue he was a historical figure based on the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, genealogical records, and references in the {{ill|Baekje Annals|ja|百済記}}. Others suggest he is a later legendary addition{{Sfn|Kagawake (Ancient Clan)|2010}}.
The phonetic similarity of "Kaga" has led to theories linking Kagawake to {{ill|Ashikaga District|ja|足利郡}}, present-day {{ill|Ashikaga|ja|足利市}}, Tochigi Prefecture. Some traditions suggest Kagawake is connected to the origin of the place name "Ashikaga." <ref>{{Cite book|title=Japanese Geographic Resources|chapter=Research on Keno Province in the Kofun Period}}</ref>
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== References == * {{Cite book|title=Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Clans: Popular Edition|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館}}|year=2010|chapter=Kagawake|isbn=978-4642014588|ref={{Harvid|Kagawake (Ancient Clan)|2010}}}}
== See Also == * {{ill|List of pre-Asuka period figures|ja|飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧}} * {{ill|Kamitsuke Clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan}}

{{Infobox Ancient Aristocrat | Name = Kagawake | Image = | Image_Size = | Image_Caption = | Era = [[Kofun period]] | Birth_Date = Unknown | Death_Date = Unknown | Alternate_Names = Kagawake no Mikoto, Kannagiwake, Kannagiwake no Mikoto, Kagawake no Ō, Kagawake no Mikoto | Posthumous_Name = | Deity_Name = | Dharma_Name = | Burial_Place = | Titles = [[Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko|First Ukita Kuni no Miyatsuko]] | Allegiances = [[Emperor Seimu]], [[Empress Jingū]], [[Emperor Ōjin]] | Clan = {{ill|Kamitsuke Clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan}}, {{ill|Shimosuke Clan|ja|下毛野氏|lt=Shimotsukeno clan}}, Ancestor of the {{ill|Kibiya Clan|ja|吉弥侯部}} | Parents = | Spouse = | Children = | Notable_Features = }} '''Kagawake''' (かがわけ{{Sfn|Kagawake (Ancient Clan)|2010}}, birth and death dates unknown) is a historical figure from the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} referenced in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} and other historical texts. He was the first '''{{ill|Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|浮田国造}}''', an ancestor of the {{ill|Kamitsuke Clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan}} and {{ill|Kibiya Clan|ja|吉弥侯部}}. Kagawake is described as a general sent to the Korean Peninsula under {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}'s reign. The "Kannagiwake" (巫別) mentioned during the {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} period is believed to be the same individual{{Sfn|Kagawake (Ancient Clan)|2010}}.
== Records ==
The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} records Kagawake's involvement in the following events: * In the 49th year of {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}, he was appointed a general alongside {{ill|Aratawake|ja|荒田別|1=Aratawake}} to subjugate {{ill|Silla|ja|新羅}}. His return to Japan is noted in the 50th year. * In the 15th year of {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}, "Kannagiwake" was dispatched with Aratawake to {{ill|Baekje|ja|百済}}. The following year, they returned with {{ill|Wani|ja|王仁}}. "Kannagiwake" is identified with Kagawake{{Sfn|Kagawake (Ancient Clan)|2010}}. This record also describes Aratawake and Kannagiwake as "ancestors of the Kamitsuke Clan." * According to the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}, "Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi" section, "Kagawake no Ō," a fifth-generation descendant of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}, was appointed as the Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko under {{ill|Emperor Seimu|ja|成務天皇}}'s reign.
The name Kagawake also appears in the {{ill|Sumiyoshi Taisha Jindaiki|ja|住吉大社神代記}} in the context of the Silla expedition.
== Analysis ==
Kagawake's existence is debated among scholars. Some argue he was a historical figure based on the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, genealogical records, and references in the {{ill|Baekje Annals|ja|百済記}}. Others suggest he is a later legendary addition{{Sfn|Kagawake (Ancient Clan)|2010}}.
The phonetic similarity of "Kaga" has led to theories linking Kagawake to {{ill|Ashikaga District|ja|足利郡}}, present-day {{ill|Ashikaga|ja|足利市}}, Tochigi Prefecture. Some traditions suggest Kagawake is connected to the origin of the place name "Ashikaga." <ref>{{Cite book|title=Japanese Geographic Resources|chapter=Research on Keno Province in the Kofun Period}}</ref>
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== References == * {{Cite book|title=Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Clans: Popular Edition|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館}}|year=2010|chapter=Kagawake|isbn=978-4642014588|ref={{Harvid|Kagawake (Ancient Clan)|2010}}}}

{{Infobox Ancient Aristocrat | Name = Kagawake | Image = | Image_Size = | Image_Caption = | Era = [[Kofun period]] | Birth_Date = Unknown | Death_Date = Unknown | Alternate_Names = Kagawake no Mikoto, Kannagiwake, Kannagiwake no Mikoto, Kagawake no Ō, Kagawake no Mikoto | Posthumous_Name = | Deity_Name = | Dharma_Name = | Burial_Place = | Titles = [[Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko|First Ukita Kuni no Miyatsuko]] | Allegiances = [[Emperor Seimu]], [[Empress Jingū]], [[Emperor Ōjin]] | Clan = {{ill|Kamitsuke Clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan}}, {{ill|Shimosuke Clan|ja|下毛野氏|lt=Shimotsukeno clan}}, Ancestor of the {{ill|Kibiya Clan|ja|吉弥侯部}} | Parents = | Spouse = | Children = | Notable_Features = }} '''Kagawake''' (かがわけ{{Sfn|Kagawake (Ancient Clan)|2010}}, birth and death dates unknown) is a historical figure from the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} referenced in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} and other historical texts. He was the first '''{{ill|Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|浮田国造}}''', an ancestor of the {{ill|Kamitsuke Clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan}} and {{ill|Kibiya Clan|ja|吉弥侯部}}. Kagawake is described as a general sent to the Korean Peninsula under {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}'s reign. The "Kannagiwake" (巫別) mentioned during the {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} period is believed to be the same individual{{Sfn|Kagawake (Ancient Clan)|2010}}.
== Records ==
The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} records Kagawake's involvement in the following events: * In the 49th year of {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}, he was appointed a general alongside {{ill|Aratawake|ja|荒田別|1=Aratawake}} to subjugate {{ill|Silla|ja|新羅}}. His return to Japan is noted in the 50th year. * In the 15th year of {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}, "Kannagiwake" was dispatched with Aratawake to {{ill|Baekje|ja|百済}}. The following year, they returned with {{ill|Wani|ja|王仁}}. "Kannagiwake" is identified with Kagawake{{Sfn|Kagawake (Ancient Clan)|2010}}. This record also describes Aratawake and Kannagiwake as "ancestors of the Kamitsuke Clan." * According to the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}, "Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi" section, "Kagawake no Ō," a fifth-generation descendant of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}, was appointed as the Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko under {{ill|Emperor Seimu|ja|成務天皇}}'s reign.
The name Kagawake also appears in the {{ill|Sumiyoshi Taisha Jindaiki|ja|住吉大社神代記}} in the context of the Silla expedition.
== Analysis ==
Kagawake's existence is debated among scholars. Some argue he was a historical figure based on the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, genealogical records, and references in the {{ill|Baekje Annals|ja|百済記}}. Others suggest he is a later legendary addition{{Sfn|Kagawake (Ancient Clan)|2010}}.
The phonetic similarity of "Kaga" has led to theories linking Kagawake to {{ill|Ashikaga District|ja|足利郡}}, present-day {{ill|Ashikaga|ja|足利市}}, Tochigi Prefecture. Some traditions suggest Kagawake is connected to the origin of the place name "Ashikaga." <ref>{{Cite book|title=Japanese Geographic Resources|chapter=Research on Keno Province in the Kofun Period}}</ref>
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== References == * {{Cite book|title=Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Clans: Popular Edition|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館}}|year=2010|chapter=Kagawake|isbn=978-4642014588|ref={{Harvid|Kagawake (Ancient Clan)|2010}}}}
== See Also == * {{ill|List of pre-Asuka period figures|ja|飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧}} * {{ill|Kamitsuke Clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan}}

{{Infobox Ancient Aristocrat | Name = Kagawake | Image = | Image_Size = | Image_Caption = | Era = [[Kofun period]] | Birth_Date = Unknown | Death_Date = Unknown | Alternate_Names = Kagawake no Mikoto, Kannagiwake, Kannagiwake no Mikoto, Kagawake no Ō, Kagawake no Mikoto | Posthumous_Name = | Deity_Name = | Dharma_Name = | Burial_Place = | Titles = [[Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko|First Ukita Kuni no Miyatsuko]] | Allegiances = [[Emperor Seimu]], [[Empress Jingū]], [[Emperor Ōjin]] | Clan = {{ill|Kamitsuke Clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan}}, {{ill|Shimosuke Clan|ja|下毛野氏|lt=Shimotsukeno clan}}, Ancestor of the {{ill|Kibiya Clan|ja|吉弥侯部}} | Parents = | Spouse = | Children = | Notable_Features = }} '''Kagawake''' (かがわけ{{Sfn|Kagawake (Ancient Clan)|2010}}, birth and death dates unknown) is a historical figure from the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} referenced in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} and other historical texts. He was the first '''{{ill|Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|浮田国造}}''', an ancestor of the {{ill|Kamitsuke Clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan}} and {{ill|Kibiya Clan|ja|吉弥侯部}}. Kagawake is described as a general sent to the Korean Peninsula under {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}'s reign. The "Kannagiwake" (巫別) mentioned during the {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} period is believed to be the same individual{{Sfn|Kagawake (Ancient Clan)|2010}}.
== Records ==
The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} records Kagawake's involvement in the following events: * In the 49th year of {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}, he was appointed a general alongside {{ill|Aratawake|ja|荒田別|1=Aratawake}} to subjugate {{ill|Silla|ja|新羅}}. His return to Japan is noted in the 50th year. * In the 15th year of {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}, "Kannagiwake" was dispatched with Aratawake to {{ill|Baekje|ja|百済}}. The following year, they returned with {{ill|Wani|ja|王仁}}. "Kannagiwake" is identified with Kagawake{{Sfn|Kagawake (Ancient Clan)|2010}}. This record also describes Aratawake and Kannagiwake as "ancestors of the Kamitsuke Clan." * According to the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}, "Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi" section, "Kagawake no Ō," a fifth-generation descendant of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}, was appointed as the Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko under {{ill|Emperor Seimu|ja|成務天皇}}'s reign.
The name Kagawake also appears in the {{ill|Sumiyoshi Taisha Jindaiki|ja|住吉大社神代記}} in the context of the Silla expedition.
== Analysis ==
Kagawake's existence is debated among scholars. Some argue he was a historical figure based on the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, genealogical records, and references in the {{ill|Baekje Annals|ja|百済記}}. Others suggest he is a later legendary addition{{Sfn|Kagawake (Ancient Clan)|2010}}.
The phonetic similarity of "Kaga" has led to theories linking Kagawake to {{ill|Ashikaga District|ja|足利郡}}, present-day {{ill|Ashikaga|ja|足利市}}, Tochigi Prefecture. Some traditions suggest Kagawake is connected to the origin of the place name "Ashikaga." <ref>{{Cite book|title=Japanese Geographic Resources|chapter=Research on Keno Province in the Kofun Period}}</ref>
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== References == * {{Cite book|title=Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Clans: Popular Edition|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館}}|year=2010|chapter=Kagawake|isbn=978-4642014588|ref={{Harvid|Kagawake (Ancient Clan)|2010}}}}
== See Also == * {{ill|List of pre-Asuka period figures|ja|飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧}} * {{ill|Kamitsuke Clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan}}

His return to Japan is noted in the 50th year. * In the 15th year of {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}, "Kannagiwake" was dispatched with Aratawake to {{ill|Baekje|ja|百済}}. The following year, they returned with {{ill|Wani|ja|王仁}}. "Kannagiwake" is identified with Kagawake{{Sfn|Kagawake (Ancient Clan)|2010}}. This record also describes Aratawake and Kannagiwake as "ancestors of the Kamitsuke Clan." * According to the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}, "Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi" section, "Kagawake no Ō," a fifth-generation descendant of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}, was appointed as the Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko under {{ill|Emperor Seimu|ja|成務天皇}}'s reign.
The name Kagawake also appears in the {{ill|Sumiyoshi Taisha Jindaiki|ja|住吉大社神代記}} in the context of the Silla expedition.
== Analysis ==
Kagawake's existence is debated among scholars. Some argue he was a historical figure based on the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, genealogical records, and references in the {{ill|Baekje Annals|ja|百済記}}. Others suggest he is a later legendary addition{{Sfn|Kagawake (Ancient Clan)|2010}}.
The phonetic similarity of "Kaga" has led to theories linking Kagawake to {{ill|Ashikaga District|ja|足利郡}}, present-day {{ill|Ashikaga|ja|足利市}}, Tochigi Prefecture. Some traditions suggest Kagawake is connected to the origin of the place name "Ashikaga." <ref>{{Cite book|title=Japanese Geographic Resources|chapter=Research on Keno Province in the Kofun Period}}</ref>
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== References == * {{Cite book|title=Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Clans: Popular Edition|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館}}|year=2010|chapter=Kagawake|isbn=978-4642014588|ref={{Harvid|Kagawake (Ancient Clan)|2010}}}}

revision.text:
{{Infobox Ancient Aristocrat | Name = Kagawake | Image = | Image_Size = | Image_Caption = | Era = [[Kofun period]] | Birth_Date = Unknown | Death_Date = Unknown | Alternate_Names = Kagawake no Mikoto, Kannagiwake, Kannagiwake no Mikoto, Kagawake no Ō, Kagawake no Mikoto | Posthumous_Name = | Deity_Name = | Dharma_Name = | Burial_Place = | Titles = [[Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko|First Ukita Kuni no Miyatsuko]] | Allegiances = [[Emperor Seimu]], [[Empress Jingū]], [[Emperor Ōjin]] | Clan = {{ill|Kamitsuke Clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan}}, {{ill|Shimosuke Clan|ja|下毛野氏|lt=Shimotsukeno clan}}, Ancestor of the {{ill|Kibiya Clan|ja|吉弥侯部}} | Parents = | Spouse = | Children = | Notable_Features = }} '''Kagawake''' (かがわけ{{Sfn|Kagawake (Ancient Clan)|2010}}, birth and death dates unknown) is a historical figure from the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} referenced in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} and other historical texts. He was the first '''{{ill|Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|浮田国造}}''', an ancestor of the {{ill|Kamitsuke Clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan}} and {{ill|Kibiya Clan|ja|吉弥侯部}}. Kagawake is described as a general sent to the Korean Peninsula under {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}'s reign. The "Kannagiwake" (巫別) mentioned during the {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} period is believed to be the same individual{{Sfn|Kagawake (Ancient Clan)|2010}}.
== Records ==
The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} records Kagawake's involvement in the following events: * In the 49th year of {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}, he was appointed a general alongside {{ill|Aratawake|ja|荒田別|1=Aratawake}} to subjugate {{ill|Silla|ja|新羅}}. His return to Japan is noted in the 50th year. * In the 15th year of {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}, "Kannagiwake" was dispatched with Aratawake to {{ill|Baekje|ja|百済}}. The following year, they returned with {{ill|Wani|ja|王仁}}. "Kannagiwake" is identified with Kagawake{{Sfn|Kagawake (Ancient Clan)|2010}}. This record also describes Aratawake and Kannagiwake as "ancestors of the Kamitsuke Clan." * According to the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}, "Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi" section, "Kagawake no Ō," a fifth-generation descendant of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}, was appointed as the Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko under {{ill|Emperor Seimu|ja|成務天皇}}'s reign.
The name Kagawake also appears in the {{ill|Sumiyoshi Taisha Jindaiki|ja|住吉大社神代記}} in the context of the Silla expedition.
== Analysis ==
Kagawake's existence is debated among scholars. Some argue he was a historical figure based on the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, genealogical records, and references in the {{ill|Baekje Annals|ja|百済記}}. Others suggest he is a later legendary addition{{Sfn|Kagawake (Ancient Clan)|2010}}.
The phonetic similarity of "Kaga" has led to theories linking Kagawake to {{ill|Ashikaga District|ja|足利郡}}, present-day {{ill|Ashikaga|ja|足利市}}, Tochigi Prefecture. Some traditions suggest Kagawake is connected to the origin of the place name "Ashikaga." <ref>{{Cite book|title=Japanese Geographic Resources|chapter=Research on Keno Province in the Kofun Period}}</ref>
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== References == * {{Cite book|title=Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Clans: Popular Edition|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館}}|year=2010|chapter=Kagawake|isbn=978-4642014588|ref={{Harvid|Kagawake (Ancient Clan)|2010}}}}
