{{Infobox Ancient Aristocrat | Name = Kagawake | Image = | Image_Size = | Image_Caption = | Era = [[Kofun period]] | Birth_Date = Unknown | Death_Date = Unknown | Alternate_Names = Kagawake no Mikoto, Kannagiwake, Kannagiwake no Mikoto, Kagawake no Ō, Kagawake no Mikoto | Posthumous_Name = | Deity_Name = | Dharma_Name = | Burial_Place = | Titles = [[Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko|First Ukita Kuni no Miyatsuko]] | Allegiances = [[Emperor Seimu]], [[Empress Jingū]], [[Emperor Ōjin]] | Clan = {{ill|Kamitsuke Clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan}}, {{ill|Shimosuke Clan|ja|下毛野氏|lt=Shimotsukeno clan}}, Ancestor of the {{ill|Kibiya Clan|ja|吉弥侯部}} | Parents = | Spouse = | Children = | Notable_Features = }} '''Kagawake''' (かがわけ{{Sfn|Kagawake (Ancient Clan)|2010}}, birth and death dates unknown) is a historical figure from the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} referenced in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} and other historical texts. He was the first '''{{ill|Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|浮田国造}}''', an ancestor of the {{ill|Kamitsuke Clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan}} and {{ill|Kibiya Clan|ja|吉弥侯部}}. Kagawake is described as a general sent to the Korean Peninsula under {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}'s reign. The "Kannagiwake" (巫別) mentioned during the {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} period is believed to be the same individual{{Sfn|Kagawake (Ancient Clan)|2010}}.
== Records ==
The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} records Kagawake's involvement in the following events: * In the 49th year of {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}, he was appointed a general alongside {{ill|Aratawake|ja|荒田別|1=Aratawake}} to subjugate {{ill|Silla|ja|新羅}}. His return to Japan is noted in the 50th year. * In the 15th year of {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}, "Kannagiwake" was dispatched with Aratawake to {{ill|Baekje|ja|百済}}. The following year, they returned with {{ill|Wani|ja|王仁}}. "Kannagiwake" is identified with Kagawake{{Sfn|Kagawake (Ancient Clan)|2010}}. This record also describes Aratawake and Kannagiwake as "ancestors of the Kamitsuke Clan." * According to the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}, "Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi" section, "Kagawake no Ō," a fifth-generation descendant of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}, was appointed as the Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko under {{ill|Emperor Seimu|ja|成務天皇}}'s reign.
The name Kagawake also appears in the {{ill|Sumiyoshi Taisha Jindaiki|ja|住吉大社神代記}} in the context of the Silla expedition.
== Analysis ==
Kagawake's existence is debated among scholars. Some argue he was a historical figure based on the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, genealogical records, and references in the {{ill|Baekje Annals|ja|百済記}}. Others suggest he is a later legendary addition{{Sfn|Kagawake (Ancient Clan)|2010}}.
The phonetic similarity of "Kaga" has led to theories linking Kagawake to {{ill|Ashikaga District|ja|足利郡}}, present-day {{ill|Ashikaga|ja|足利市}}, Tochigi Prefecture. Some traditions suggest Kagawake is connected to the origin of the place name "Ashikaga." <ref>{{Cite book|title=Japanese Geographic Resources|chapter=Research on Keno Province in the Kofun Period}}</ref>
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== References == * {{Cite book|title=Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Clans: Popular Edition|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館}}|year=2010|chapter=Kagawake|isbn=978-4642014588|ref={{Harvid|Kagawake (Ancient Clan)|2010}}}}
== See Also == * {{ill|List of pre-Asuka period figures|ja|飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧}} * {{ill|Kamitsuke Clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan}}

revision.comment: Text replacement - "qq" to "11"
revision.text:
{{Infobox Ancient Aristocrat | Name = Kagawake | Image = | Image_Size = | Image_Caption = | Era = [[Kofun period]] | Birth_Date = Unknown | Death_Date = Unknown | Alternate_Names = Kagawake no Mikoto, Kannagiwake, Kannagiwake no Mikoto, Kagawake no Ō, Kagawake no Mikoto | Posthumous_Name = | Deity_Name = | Dharma_Name = | Burial_Place = | Titles = [[Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko|First Ukita Kuni no Miyatsuko]] | Allegiances = [[Emperor Seimu]], [[Empress Jingū]], [[Emperor Ōjin]] | Clan = {{ill|Kamitsuke Clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan}}, {{ill|Shimosuke Clan|ja|下毛野氏|lt=Shimotsukeno clan}}, Ancestor of the {{ill|Kibiya Clan|ja|吉弥侯部}} | Parents = | Spouse = | Children = | Notable_Features = }} '''Kagawake''' (かがわけ{{Sfn|Kagawake (Ancient Clan)|2010}}, birth and death dates unknown) is a historical figure from the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} referenced in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} and other historical texts. He was the first '''{{ill|Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|浮田国造}}''', an ancestor of the {{ill|Kamitsuke Clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan}} and {{ill|Kibiya Clan|ja|吉弥侯部}}. Kagawake is described as a general sent to the Korean Peninsula under {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}'s reign. The "Kannagiwake" (巫別) mentioned during the {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} period is believed to be the same individual{{Sfn|Kagawake (Ancient Clan)|2010}}.
== Records ==
The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} records Kagawake's involvement in the following events: * In the 49th year of {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}, he was appointed a general alongside {{ill|Aratawake|ja|荒田別|1=Aratawake}} to subjugate {{ill|Silla|ja|新羅}}. His return to Japan is noted in the 50th year. * In the 15th year of {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}, "Kannagiwake" was dispatched with Aratawake to {{ill|Baekje|ja|百済}}. The following year, they returned with {{ill|Wani|ja|王仁}}. "Kannagiwake" is identified with Kagawake{{Sfn|Kagawake (Ancient Clan)|2010}}. This record also describes Aratawake and Kannagiwake as "ancestors of the Kamitsuke Clan." * According to the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}, "Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi" section, "Kagawake no Ō," a fifth-generation descendant of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}, was appointed as the Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko under {{ill|Emperor Seimu|ja|成務天皇}}'s reign.
The name Kagawake also appears in the {{ill|Sumiyoshi Taisha Jindaiki|ja|住吉大社神代記}} in the context of the Silla expedition.
== Analysis ==
Kagawake's existence is debated among scholars. Some argue he was a historical figure based on the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, genealogical records, and references in the {{ill|Baekje Annals|ja|百済記}}. Others suggest he is a later legendary addition{{Sfn|Kagawake (Ancient Clan)|2010}}.
The phonetic similarity of "Kaga" has led to theories linking Kagawake to {{ill|Ashikaga District|ja|足利郡}}, present-day {{ill|Ashikaga|ja|足利市}}, Tochigi Prefecture. Some traditions suggest Kagawake is connected to the origin of the place name "Ashikaga." <ref>{{Cite book|title=Japanese Geographic Resources|chapter=Research on Keno Province in the Kofun Period}}</ref>
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== References == * {{Cite book|title=Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Clans: Popular Edition|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館}}|year=2010|chapter=Kagawake|isbn=978-4642014588|ref={{Harvid|Kagawake (Ancient Clan)|2010}}}}
== See Also == * {{ill|List of pre-Asuka period figures|ja|飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧}} * {{ill|Kamitsuke Clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan}}

{{Infobox Ancient Aristocrat | Name = Kagawake | image = | image_Size = | image_Caption = | Era = [[Kofun period]] | Birth_Date = Unknown | Death_Date = Unknown | Alternate_Names = Kagawake no Mikoto, Kannagiwake, Kannagiwake no Mikoto, Kagawake no Ō, Kagawake no Mikoto | Posthumous_Name = | Deity_Name = | Dharma_Name = | Burial_Place = | Titles = [[Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko|First Ukita Kuni no Miyatsuko]] | Allegiances = [[Emperor Seimu]], [[Empress Jingū]], [[Emperor Ōjin]] | Clan = {{ill|Kamitsuke Clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan}}, {{ill|Shimosuke Clan|ja|下毛野氏|lt=Shimotsukeno clan}}, Ancestor of the {{ill|Kibiya Clan|ja|吉弥侯部}} | Parents = | Spouse = | Children = | Notable_Features = }} '''Kagawake''' (かがわけ{{Sfn|Kagawake (Ancient Clan)|2010}}, birth and death dates unknown) is a historical figure from the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} referenced in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} and other historical texts. He was the first '''{{ill|Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|浮田国造}}''', an ancestor of the {{ill|Kamitsuke Clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan}} and {{ill|Kibiya Clan|ja|吉弥侯部}}. Kagawake is described as a general sent to the Korean Peninsula under {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}'s reign. The "Kannagiwake" (巫別) mentioned during the {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} period is believed to be the same individual{{Sfn|Kagawake (Ancient Clan)|2010}}.
== Records ==
The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} records Kagawake's involvement in the following events: * In the 49th year of {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}, he was appointed a general alongside {{ill|Aratawake|ja|荒田別|1=Aratawake}} to subjugate {{ill|Silla|ja|新羅}}. His return to Japan is noted in the 50th year. * In the 15th year of {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}, "Kannagiwake" was dispatched with Aratawake to {{ill|Baekje|ja|百済}}. The following year, they returned with {{ill|Wani|ja|王仁}}. "Kannagiwake" is identified with Kagawake{{Sfn|Kagawake (Ancient Clan)|2010}}. This record also describes Aratawake and Kannagiwake as "ancestors of the Kamitsuke Clan." * According to the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}, "Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi" section, "Kagawake no Ō," a fifth-generation descendant of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}, was appointed as the Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko under {{ill|Emperor Seimu|ja|成務天皇}}'s reign.
The name Kagawake also appears in the {{ill|Sumiyoshi Taisha Jindaiki|ja|住吉大社神代記}} in the context of the Silla expedition.
== Analysis ==
Kagawake's existence is debated among scholars. Some argue he was a historical figure based on the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, genealogical records, and references in the {{ill|Baekje Annals|ja|百済記}}. Others suggest he is a later legendary addition{{Sfn|Kagawake (Ancient Clan)|2010}}.
The phonetic similarity of "Kaga" has led to theories linking Kagawake to {{ill|Ashikaga District|ja|足利郡}}, present-day {{ill|Ashikaga|ja|足利市}}, Tochigi Prefecture. Some traditions suggest Kagawake is connected to the origin of the place name "Ashikaga." <ref>{{Cite book|title=Japanese Geographic Resources|chapter=Research on Keno Province in the Kofun Period}}</ref>
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== References == * {{Cite book|title=Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Clans: Popular Edition|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館}}|year=2010|chapter=Kagawake|isbn=978-4642014588|ref={{Harvid|Kagawake (Ancient Clan)|2010}}}}
== See Also == * {{ill|List of pre-Asuka period figures|ja|飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧}} * {{ill|Kamitsuke Clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan}}

{{Infobox Ancient Aristocrat | Name = Kagawake | image = | image_Size = | image_Caption = | Era = [[Kofun period]] | Birth_Date = Unknown | Death_Date = Unknown | Alternate_Names = Kagawake no Mikoto, Kannagiwake, Kannagiwake no Mikoto, Kagawake no Ō, Kagawake no Mikoto | Posthumous_Name = | Deity_Name = | Dharma_Name = | Burial_Place = | Titles = [[Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko|First Ukita Kuni no Miyatsuko]] | Allegiances = [[Emperor Seimu]], [[Empress Jingū]], [[Emperor Ōjin]] | Clan = {{ill|Kamitsuke Clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan}}, {{ill|Shimosuke Clan|ja|下毛野氏|lt=Shimotsukeno clan}}, Ancestor of the {{ill|Kibiya Clan|ja|吉弥侯部}} | Parents = | Spouse = | Children = | Notable_Features = }} '''Kagawake''' (かがわけ{{Sfn|Kagawake (Ancient Clan)|2010}}, birth and death dates unknown) is a historical figure from the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} referenced in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} and other historical texts. He was the first '''{{ill|Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|浮田国造}}''', an ancestor of the {{ill|Kamitsuke Clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan}} and {{ill|Kibiya Clan|ja|吉弥侯部}}. Kagawake is described as a general sent to the Korean Peninsula under {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}'s reign. The "Kannagiwake" (巫別) mentioned during the {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} period is believed to be the same individual{{Sfn|Kagawake (Ancient Clan)|2010}}.
== Records ==
The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} records Kagawake's involvement in the following events: * In the 49th year of {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}, he was appointed a general alongside {{ill|Aratawake|ja|荒田別|1=Aratawake}} to subjugate {{ill|Silla|ja|新羅}}. His return to Japan is noted in the 50th year. * In the 15th year of {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}, "Kannagiwake" was dispatched with Aratawake to {{ill|Baekje|ja|百済}}. The following year, they returned with {{ill|Wani|ja|王仁}}. "Kannagiwake" is identified with Kagawake{{Sfn|Kagawake (Ancient Clan)|2010}}. This record also describes Aratawake and Kannagiwake as "ancestors of the Kamitsuke Clan." * According to the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}, "Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi" section, "Kagawake no Ō," a fifth-generation descendant of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}, was appointed as the Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko under {{ill|Emperor Seimu|ja|成務天皇}}'s reign.
The name Kagawake also appears in the {{ill|Sumiyoshi Taisha Jindaiki|ja|住吉大社神代記}} in the context of the Silla expedition.
== Analysis ==
Kagawake's existence is debated among scholars. Some argue he was a historical figure based on the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, genealogical records, and references in the {{ill|Baekje Annals|ja|百済記}}. Others suggest he is a later legendary addition{{Sfn|Kagawake (Ancient Clan)|2010}}.
The phonetic similarity of "Kaga" has led to theories linking Kagawake to {{ill|Ashikaga District|ja|足利郡}}, present-day {{ill|Ashikaga|ja|足利市}}, Tochigi Prefecture. Some traditions suggest Kagawake is connected to the origin of the place name "Ashikaga." <ref>{{Cite book|title=Japanese Geographic Resources|chapter=Research on Keno Province in the Kofun Period}}</ref>
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== References == * {{Cite book|title=Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Clans: Popular Edition|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館}}|year=2010|chapter=Kagawake|isbn=978-4642014588|ref={{Harvid|Kagawake (Ancient Clan)|2010}}}}
== See Also == * {{ill|List of pre-Asuka period figures|ja|飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧}} * {{ill|Kamitsuke Clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan}}

revision.comment: Removing from [[Category:Qq]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{Infobox Ancient Aristocrat | Name = Kagawake | image = | image_Size = | image_Caption = | Era = [[Kofun period]] | Birth_Date = Unknown | Death_Date = Unknown | Alternate_Names = Kagawake no Mikoto, Kannagiwake, Kannagiwake no Mikoto, Kagawake no Ō, Kagawake no Mikoto | Posthumous_Name = | Deity_Name = | Dharma_Name = | Burial_Place = | Titles = [[Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko|First Ukita Kuni no Miyatsuko]] | Allegiances = [[Emperor Seimu]], [[Empress Jingū]], [[Emperor Ōjin]] | Clan = {{ill|Kamitsuke Clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan}}, {{ill|Shimosuke Clan|ja|下毛野氏|lt=Shimotsukeno clan}}, Ancestor of the {{ill|Kibiya Clan|ja|吉弥侯部}} | Parents = | Spouse = | Children = | Notable_Features = }} '''Kagawake''' (かがわけ{{Sfn|Kagawake (Ancient Clan)|2010}}, birth and death dates unknown) is a historical figure from the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} referenced in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} and other historical texts. He was the first '''{{ill|Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|浮田国造}}''', an ancestor of the {{ill|Kamitsuke Clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan}} and {{ill|Kibiya Clan|ja|吉弥侯部}}. Kagawake is described as a general sent to the Korean Peninsula under {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}'s reign. The "Kannagiwake" (巫別) mentioned during the {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} period is believed to be the same individual{{Sfn|Kagawake (Ancient Clan)|2010}}.
== Records ==
The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} records Kagawake's involvement in the following events: * In the 49th year of {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}, he was appointed a general alongside {{ill|Aratawake|ja|荒田別|1=Aratawake}} to subjugate {{ill|Silla|ja|新羅}}. His return to Japan is noted in the 50th year. * In the 15th year of {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}, "Kannagiwake" was dispatched with Aratawake to {{ill|Baekje|ja|百済}}. The following year, they returned with {{ill|Wani|ja|王仁}}. "Kannagiwake" is identified with Kagawake{{Sfn|Kagawake (Ancient Clan)|2010}}. This record also describes Aratawake and Kannagiwake as "ancestors of the Kamitsuke Clan." * According to the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}, "Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi" section, "Kagawake no Ō," a fifth-generation descendant of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}, was appointed as the Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko under {{ill|Emperor Seimu|ja|成務天皇}}'s reign.
The name Kagawake also appears in the {{ill|Sumiyoshi Taisha Jindaiki|ja|住吉大社神代記}} in the context of the Silla expedition.
== Analysis ==
Kagawake's existence is debated among scholars. Some argue he was a historical figure based on the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, genealogical records, and references in the {{ill|Baekje Annals|ja|百済記}}. Others suggest he is a later legendary addition{{Sfn|Kagawake (Ancient Clan)|2010}}.
The phonetic similarity of "Kaga" has led to theories linking Kagawake to {{ill|Ashikaga District|ja|足利郡}}, present-day {{ill|Ashikaga|ja|足利市}}, Tochigi Prefecture. Some traditions suggest Kagawake is connected to the origin of the place name "Ashikaga." <ref>{{Cite book|title=Japanese Geographic Resources|chapter=Research on Keno Province in the Kofun Period}}</ref>
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== References == * {{Cite book|title=Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Clans: Popular Edition|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館}}|year=2010|chapter=Kagawake|isbn=978-4642014588|ref={{Harvid|Kagawake (Ancient Clan)|2010}}}}
== See Also == * {{ill|List of pre-Asuka period figures|ja|飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧}} * {{ill|Kamitsuke Clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan}}

{{Infobox Ancient Aristocrat | Name = Kagawake | image = | image_Size = | image_Caption = | Era = [[Kofun period]] | Birth_Date = Unknown | Death_Date = Unknown | Alternate_Names = Kagawake no Mikoto, Kannagiwake, Kannagiwake no Mikoto, Kagawake no Ō, Kagawake no Mikoto | Posthumous_Name = | Deity_Name = | Dharma_Name = | Burial_Place = | Titles = [[Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko|First Ukita Kuni no Miyatsuko]] | Allegiances = [[Emperor Seimu]], [[Empress Jingū]], [[Emperor Ōjin]] | Clan = {{ill|Kamitsuke Clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan}}, {{ill|Shimosuke Clan|ja|下毛野氏|1=Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno clan}}, Ancestor of the {{ill|Kibiya Clan|ja|吉弥侯部}} | Parents = | Spouse = | Children = | Notable_Features = }} '''Kagawake''' (かがわけ{{Sfn|Kagawake (Ancient Clan)|2010}}, birth and death dates unknown) is a historical figure from the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} referenced in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} and other historical texts. He was the first '''{{ill|Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|浮田国造}}''', an ancestor of the {{ill|Kamitsuke Clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan}} and {{ill|Kibiya Clan|ja|吉弥侯部}}. Kagawake is described as a general sent to the Korean Peninsula under {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}'s reign. The "Kannagiwake" (巫別) mentioned during the {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} period is believed to be the same individual{{Sfn|Kagawake (Ancient Clan)|2010}}.
== Records ==
The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} records Kagawake's involvement in the following events: * In the 49th year of {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}, he was appointed a general alongside {{ill|Aratawake|ja|荒田別|1=Aratawake}} to subjugate {{ill|Silla|ja|新羅}}. His return to Japan is noted in the 50th year. * In the 15th year of {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}, "Kannagiwake" was dispatched with Aratawake to {{ill|Baekje|ja|百済}}. The following year, they returned with {{ill|Wani|ja|王仁}}. "Kannagiwake" is identified with Kagawake{{Sfn|Kagawake (Ancient Clan)|2010}}. This record also describes Aratawake and Kannagiwake as "ancestors of the Kamitsuke Clan." * According to the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}, "Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi" section, "Kagawake no Ō," a fifth-generation descendant of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}, was appointed as the Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko under {{ill|Emperor Seimu|ja|成務天皇}}'s reign.
The name Kagawake also appears in the {{ill|Sumiyoshi Taisha Jindaiki|ja|住吉大社神代記}} in the context of the Silla expedition.
== Analysis ==
Kagawake's existence is debated among scholars. Some argue he was a historical figure based on the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, genealogical records, and references in the {{ill|Baekje Annals|ja|百済記}}. Others suggest he is a later legendary addition{{Sfn|Kagawake (Ancient Clan)|2010}}.
The phonetic similarity of "Kaga" has led to theories linking Kagawake to {{ill|Ashikaga District|ja|足利郡}}, present-day {{ill|Ashikaga|ja|足利市}}, Tochigi Prefecture. Some traditions suggest Kagawake is connected to the origin of the place name "Ashikaga." <ref>{{Cite book|title=Japanese Geographic Resources|chapter=Research on Keno Province in the Kofun Period}}</ref>
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== References == * {{Cite book|title=Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Clans: Popular Edition|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館}}|year=2010|chapter=Kagawake|isbn=978-4642014588|ref={{Harvid|Kagawake (Ancient Clan)|2010}}}}
== See Also == * {{ill|List of pre-Asuka period figures|ja|飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧}} * {{ill|Kamitsuke Clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan}}

{{Infobox Ancient Aristocrat | Name = Kagawake | image = | image_Size = | image_Caption = | Era = [[Kofun period]] | Birth_Date = Unknown | Death_Date = Unknown | Alternate_Names = Kagawake no Mikoto, Kannagiwake, Kannagiwake no Mikoto, Kagawake no Ō, Kagawake no Mikoto | Posthumous_Name = | Deity_Name = | Dharma_Name = | Burial_Place = | Titles = [[Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko|First Ukita Kuni no Miyatsuko]] | Allegiances = [[Emperor Seimu]], [[Empress Jingū]], [[Emperor Ōjin]] | Clan = {{ill|Kamitsuke Clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan}}, {{ill|Shimosuke Clan|ja|下毛野氏|1=Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno clan}}, Ancestor of the {{ill|Kibiya Clan|ja|吉弥侯部}} | Parents = | Spouse = | Children = | Notable_Features = }} '''Kagawake''' (かがわけ{{Sfn|Kagawake (Ancient Clan)|2010}}, birth and death dates unknown) is a historical figure from the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} referenced in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} and other historical texts. Kagawake is described as a general sent to the Korean Peninsula under {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}'s reign. The "Kannagiwake" (巫別) mentioned during the {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} period is believed to be the same individual{{Sfn|Kagawake (Ancient Clan)|2010}}.

His return to Japan is noted in the 50th year. * In the 15th year of {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}, "Kannagiwake" was dispatched with Aratawake to {{ill|Baekje|ja|百済}}. The following year, they returned with {{ill|Wani|ja|王仁}}. "Kannagiwake" is identified with Kagawake{{Sfn|Kagawake (Ancient Clan)|2010}}. This record also describes Aratawake and Kannagiwake as "ancestors of the Kamitsuke Clan." * According to the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}, "Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi" section, "Kagawake no Ō," a fifth-generation descendant of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}, was appointed as the Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko under {{ill|Emperor Seimu|ja|成務天皇}}'s reign.
The name Kagawake also appears in the {{ill|Sumiyoshi Taisha Jindaiki|ja|住吉大社神代記}} in the context of the Silla expedition.
== Analysis ==
Kagawake's existence is debated among scholars. Some argue he was a historical figure based on the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, genealogical records, and references in the {{ill|Baekje Annals|ja|百済記}}. Others suggest he is a later legendary addition{{Sfn|Kagawake (Ancient Clan)|2010}}.
The phonetic similarity of "Kaga" has led to theories linking Kagawake to {{ill|Ashikaga District|ja|足利郡}}, present-day {{ill|Ashikaga|ja|足利市}}, Tochigi Prefecture. Some traditions suggest Kagawake is connected to the origin of the place name "Ashikaga." <ref>{{Cite book|title=Japanese Geographic Resources|chapter=Research on Keno Province in the Kofun Period}}</ref>
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== References == * {{Cite book|title=Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Clans: Popular Edition|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館}}|year=2010|chapter=Kagawake|isbn=978-4642014588|ref={{Harvid|Kagawake (Ancient Clan)|2010}}}}== See Also == * {{ill|List of pre-Asuka period figures|ja|飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧}} * {{ill|Kamitsuke Clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan}}

{{Infobox Ancient Aristocrat | Name = Kagawake | image = | image_Size = | image_Caption = | Era = [[Kofun period]] | Birth_Date = Unknown | Death_Date = Unknown | Alternate_Names = Kagawake no Mikoto, Kannagiwake, Kannagiwake no Mikoto, Kagawake no Ō, Kagawake no Mikoto | Posthumous_Name = | Deity_Name = | Dharma_Name = | Burial_Place = | Titles = [[Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko|First Ukita Kuni no Miyatsuko]] | Allegiances = [[Emperor Seimu]], [[Empress Jingū]], [[Emperor Ōjin]] | Clan = {{ill|Kamitsuke Clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan}}, {{ill|Shimosuke Clan|ja|下毛野氏|1=Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno clan}}, Ancestor of the {{ill|Kibiya Clan|ja|吉弥侯部}} | Parents = | Spouse = | Children = | Notable_Features = }} '''Kagawake''' (かがわけ{{Sfn|Kagawake (Ancient Clan)|2010}}, birth and death dates unknown) is a historical figure from the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} referenced in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} and other historical texts. He was the first '''{{ill|Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|浮田国造}}''', an ancestor of the {{ill|Kamitsuke Clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan}} and {{ill|Kibiya Clan|ja|吉弥侯部}}. Kagawake is described as a general sent to the Korean Peninsula under {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}'s reign. The "Kannagiwake" (巫別) mentioned during the {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} period is believed to be the same individual{{Sfn|Kagawake (Ancient Clan)|2010}}.
== Records ==
The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} records Kagawake's involvement in the following events: * In the 49th year of {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}, he was appointed a general alongside {{ill|Aratawake|ja|荒田別|1=Aratawake}} to subjugate {{ill|Silla|ja|新羅}}. His return to Japan is noted in the 50th year. * In the 15th year of {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}, "Kannagiwake" was dispatched with Aratawake to {{ill|Baekje|ja|百済}}. The following year, they returned with {{ill|Wani|ja|王仁}}. "Kannagiwake" is identified with Kagawake{{Sfn|Kagawake (Ancient Clan)|2010}}. This record also describes Aratawake and Kannagiwake as "ancestors of the Kamitsuke Clan." * According to the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}, "Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi" section, "Kagawake no Ō," a fifth-generation descendant of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}, was appointed as the Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko under {{ill|Emperor Seimu|ja|成務天皇}}'s reign.
The name Kagawake also appears in the {{ill|Sumiyoshi Taisha Jindaiki|ja|住吉大社神代記}} in the context of the Silla expedition.
== Analysis ==
Kagawake's existence is debated among scholars. Some argue he was a historical figure based on the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, genealogical records, and references in the {{ill|Baekje Annals|ja|百済記}}. Others suggest he is a later legendary addition{{Sfn|Kagawake (Ancient Clan)|2010}}.
The phonetic similarity of "Kaga" has led to theories linking Kagawake to {{ill|Ashikaga District|ja|足利郡}}, present-day {{ill|Ashikaga|ja|足利市}}, Tochigi Prefecture. Some traditions suggest Kagawake is connected to the origin of the place name "Ashikaga." <ref>{{Cite book|title=Japanese Geographic Resources|chapter=Research on Keno Province in the Kofun Period}}</ref>
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== References == * {{Cite book|title=Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Clans: Popular Edition|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館}}|year=2010|chapter=Kagawake|isbn=978-4642014588|ref={{Harvid|Kagawake (Ancient Clan)|2010}}}}== See Also == * {{ill|List of pre-Asuka period figures|ja|飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧}} * {{ill|Kamitsuke Clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan}}

revision.comment: Text replacement - "== See Also! ==" to "== See Also =="
revision.text:
{{Infobox Ancient Aristocrat | Name = Kagawake | image = | image_Size = | image_Caption = | Era = [[Kofun period]] | Birth_Date = Unknown | Death_Date = Unknown | Alternate_Names = Kagawake no Mikoto, Kannagiwake, Kannagiwake no Mikoto, Kagawake no Ō, Kagawake no Mikoto | Posthumous_Name = | Deity_Name = | Dharma_Name = | Burial_Place = | Titles = [[Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko|First Ukita Kuni no Miyatsuko]] | Allegiances = [[Emperor Seimu]], [[Empress Jingū]], [[Emperor Ōjin]] | Clan = {{ill|Kamitsuke Clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan}}, {{ill|Shimosuke Clan|ja|下毛野氏|1=Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno clan}}, Ancestor of the {{ill|Kibiya Clan|ja|吉弥侯部}} | Parents = | Spouse = | Children = | Notable_Features = }} '''Kagawake''' (かがわけ{{Sfn|Kagawake (Ancient Clan)|2010}}, birth and death dates unknown) is a historical figure from the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} referenced in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} and other historical texts. He was the first '''{{ill|Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|浮田国造}}''', an ancestor of the {{ill|Kamitsuke Clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan}} and {{ill|Kibiya Clan|ja|吉弥侯部}}. Kagawake is described as a general sent to the Korean Peninsula under {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}'s reign. The "Kannagiwake" (巫別) mentioned during the {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} period is believed to be the same individual{{Sfn|Kagawake (Ancient Clan)|2010}}.
== Records ==
The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} records Kagawake's involvement in the following events: * In the 49th year of {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}, he was appointed a general alongside {{ill|Aratawake|ja|荒田別|1=Aratawake}} to subjugate {{ill|Silla|ja|新羅}}. His return to Japan is noted in the 50th year. * In the 15th year of {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}, "Kannagiwake" was dispatched with Aratawake to {{ill|Baekje|ja|百済}}. The following year, they returned with {{ill|Wani|ja|王仁}}. "Kannagiwake" is identified with Kagawake{{Sfn|Kagawake (Ancient Clan)|2010}}. This record also describes Aratawake and Kannagiwake as "ancestors of the Kamitsuke Clan." * According to the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}, "Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi" section, "Kagawake no Ō," a fifth-generation descendant of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}, was appointed as the Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko under {{ill|Emperor Seimu|ja|成務天皇}}'s reign.
The name Kagawake also appears in the {{ill|Sumiyoshi Taisha Jindaiki|ja|住吉大社神代記}} in the context of the Silla expedition.
== Analysis ==
Kagawake's existence is debated among scholars. Some argue he was a historical figure based on the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, genealogical records, and references in the {{ill|Baekje Annals|ja|百済記}}. Others suggest he is a later legendary addition{{Sfn|Kagawake (Ancient Clan)|2010}}.
The phonetic similarity of "Kaga" has led to theories linking Kagawake to {{ill|Ashikaga District|ja|足利郡}}, present-day {{ill|Ashikaga|ja|足利市}}, Tochigi Prefecture. Some traditions suggest Kagawake is connected to the origin of the place name "Ashikaga." <ref>{{Cite book|title=Japanese Geographic Resources|chapter=Research on Keno Province in the Kofun Period}}</ref>
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== References == * {{Cite book|title=Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Clans: Popular Edition|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館}}|year=2010|chapter=Kagawake|isbn=978-4642014588|ref={{Harvid|Kagawake (Ancient Clan)|2010}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of pre-Asuka period figures|ja|飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧}} * {{ill|Kamitsuke Clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan}}

revision.text:
{{Infobox Ancient Aristocrat | Name = Kagawake | image = | image_Size = | image_Caption = | Era = [[Kofun period]] | Birth_Date = Unknown | Death_Date = Unknown | Alternate_Names = Kagawake no Mikoto, Kannagiwake, Kannagiwake no Mikoto, Kagawake no Ō, Kagawake no Mikoto | Posthumous_Name = | Deity_Name = | Dharma_Name = | Burial_Place = | Titles = [[Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko|First Ukita Kuni no Miyatsuko]] | Allegiances = [[Emperor Seimu]], [[Empress Jingū]], [[Emperor Ōjin]] | Clan = {{ill|Kamitsuke Clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan}}, {{ill|Shimosuke Clan|ja|下毛野氏|1=Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno clan}}, Ancestor of the {{ill|Kibiya Clan|ja|吉弥侯部}} | Parents = | Spouse = | Children = | Notable_Features = }} '''Kagawake''' (かがわけ{{Sfn|Kagawake (Ancient Clan)|2010}}, birth and death dates unknown) is a historical figure from the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} referenced in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} and other historical texts. He was the first '''{{ill|Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|浮田国造}}''', an ancestor of the {{ill|Kamitsuke Clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan}} and {{ill|Kibiya Clan|ja|吉弥侯部}}. Kagawake is described as a general sent to the Korean Peninsula under {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}'s reign. The "Kannagiwake" (巫別) mentioned during the {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} period is believed to be the same individual{{Sfn|Kagawake (Ancient Clan)|2010}}.
== Records ==
The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} records Kagawake's involvement in the following events: * In the 49th year of {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}, he was appointed a general alongside {{ill|Aratawake|ja|荒田別|1=Aratawake}} to subjugate {{ill|Silla|ja|新羅}}. His return to Japan is noted in the 50th year. * In the 15th year of {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}, "Kannagiwake" was dispatched with Aratawake to {{ill|Baekje|ja|百済}}. The following year, they returned with {{ill|Wani|ja|王仁}}. "Kannagiwake" is identified with Kagawake{{Sfn|Kagawake (Ancient Clan)|2010}}. This record also describes Aratawake and Kannagiwake as "ancestors of the Kamitsuke Clan." * According to the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}, "Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi" section, "Kagawake no Ō," a fifth-generation descendant of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}, was appointed as the Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko under {{ill|Emperor Seimu|ja|成務天皇}}'s reign.
The name Kagawake also appears in the {{ill|Sumiyoshi Taisha Jindaiki|ja|住吉大社神代記}} in the context of the Silla expedition.
== Analysis ==
Kagawake's existence is debated among scholars. Some argue he was a historical figure based on the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, genealogical records, and references in the {{ill|Baekje Annals|ja|百済記}}. Others suggest he is a later legendary addition{{Sfn|Kagawake (Ancient Clan)|2010}}.
The phonetic similarity of "Kaga" has led to theories linking Kagawake to {{ill|Ashikaga District|ja|足利郡}}, present-day {{ill|Ashikaga|ja|足利市}}, Tochigi Prefecture. Some traditions suggest Kagawake is connected to the origin of the place name "Ashikaga." <ref>{{Cite book|title=Japanese Geographic Resources|chapter=Research on Keno Province in the Kofun Period}}</ref>
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== References == * {{Cite book|title=Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Clans: Popular Edition|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館}}|year=2010|chapter=Kagawake|isbn=978-4642014588|ref={{Harvid|Kagawake (Ancient Clan)|2010}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of pre-Asuka period figures|ja|飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧}} * {{ill|Kamitsuke Clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan}}

{{Infobox Ancient Aristocrat | Name = Kagawake | image = | image_Size = | image_Caption = | Era = [[Kofun period]] | Birth_Date = Unknown | Death_Date = Unknown | Alternate_Names = Kagawake no Mikoto, Kannagiwake, Kannagiwake no Mikoto, Kagawake no Ō, Kagawake no Mikoto | Posthumous_Name = | Deity_Name = | Dharma_Name = | Burial_Place = | Titles = [[Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko|First Ukita Kuni no Miyatsuko]] | Allegiances = [[Emperor Seimu]], [[Empress Jingū]], [[Emperor Ōjin]] | Clan = {{ill|Kamitsuke Clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|12=draft}}, {{ill|Shimosuke Clan|ja|下毛野氏|1=Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno clan|12=draft}}, Ancestor of the {{ill|Kibiya Clan|ja|吉弥侯部|12=draft}} | Parents = | Spouse = | Children = | Notable_Features = }} '''Kagawake''' (かがわけ{{Sfn|Kagawake (Ancient Clan)|2010}}, birth and death dates unknown) is a historical figure from the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft}} referenced in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|12=draft}} and other historical texts. The "Kannagiwake" (巫別) mentioned during the {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇|12=draft}} period is believed to be the same individual{{Sfn|Kagawake (Ancient Clan)|2010}}.
== Records ==
The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|12=draft}} records Kagawake's involvement in the following events: * In the 49th year of {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后|12=draft}}, he was appointed a general alongside {{ill|Aratawake|ja|荒田別|1=Aratawake|12=draft}} to subjugate {{ill|Silla|ja|新羅|12=draft}}. His return to Japan is noted in the 50th year. * In the 15th year of {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇|12=draft}}, "Kannagiwake" was dispatched with Aratawake to {{ill|Baekje|ja|百済|12=draft}}. The following year, they returned with {{ill|Wani|ja|王仁|12=draft}}. "Kannagiwake" is identified with Kagawake{{Sfn|Kagawake (Ancient Clan)|2010}}. This record also describes Aratawake and Kannagiwake as "ancestors of the Kamitsuke Clan." * According to the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀|12=draft}}, "Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi" section, "Kagawake no Ō," a fifth-generation descendant of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇|12=draft}}, was appointed as the Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko under {{ill|Emperor Seimu|ja|成務天皇|12=draft}}'s reign.
The name Kagawake also appears in the {{ill|Sumiyoshi Taisha Jindaiki|ja|住吉大社神代記|12=draft}} in the context of the Silla expedition.
== Analysis ==
Kagawake's existence is debated among scholars. Some argue he was a historical figure based on the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|12=draft}}, genealogical records, and references in the {{ill|Baekje Annals|ja|百済記|12=draft}}. Others suggest he is a later legendary addition{{Sfn|Kagawake (Ancient Clan)|2010}}.
The phonetic similarity of "Kaga" has led to theories linking Kagawake to {{ill|Ashikaga District|ja|足利郡|12=draft}}, present-day {{ill|Ashikaga|ja|足利市|12=draft}}, Tochigi Prefecture. Some traditions suggest Kagawake is connected to the origin of the place name "Ashikaga." <ref>{{Cite book|title=Japanese Geographic Resources|chapter=Research on Keno Province in the Kofun Period}}</ref>

revision.text:
{{Infobox Ancient Aristocrat | Name = Kagawake | image = | image_Size = | image_Caption = | Era = [[Kofun period]] | Birth_Date = Unknown | Death_Date = Unknown | Alternate_Names = Kagawake no Mikoto, Kannagiwake, Kannagiwake no Mikoto, Kagawake no Ō, Kagawake no Mikoto | Posthumous_Name = | Deity_Name = | Dharma_Name = | Burial_Place = | Titles = [[Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko|First Ukita Kuni no Miyatsuko]] | Allegiances = [[Emperor Seimu]], [[Empress Jingū]], [[Emperor Ōjin]] | Clan = {{ill|Kamitsuke Clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Shimosuke Clan|ja|下毛野氏|1=Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno clan|12=draft|12=draft}}, Ancestor of the {{ill|Kibiya Clan|ja|吉弥侯部|12=draft|12=draft}} | Parents = | Spouse = | Children = | Notable_Features = }} '''Kagawake''' (かがわけ{{Sfn|Kagawake (Ancient Clan)|2010}}, birth and death dates unknown) is a historical figure from the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft|12=draft}} referenced in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|12=draft|12=draft}} and other historical texts. He was the first '''{{ill|Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|浮田国造|12=draft|12=draft}}''', an ancestor of the {{ill|Kamitsuke Clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Kibiya Clan|ja|吉弥侯部|12=draft|12=draft}}. Kagawake is described as a general sent to the Korean Peninsula under {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后|12=draft|12=draft}}'s reign. The "Kannagiwake" (巫別) mentioned during the {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇|12=draft|12=draft}} period is believed to be the same individual{{Sfn|Kagawake (Ancient Clan)|2010}}.
== Records ==
The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|12=draft|12=draft}} records Kagawake's involvement in the following events: * In the 49th year of {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后|12=draft|12=draft}}, he was appointed a general alongside {{ill|Aratawake|ja|荒田別|1=Aratawake|12=draft|12=draft}} to subjugate {{ill|Silla|ja|新羅|12=draft|12=draft}}. His return to Japan is noted in the 50th year. * In the 15th year of {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇|12=draft|12=draft}}, "Kannagiwake" was dispatched with Aratawake to {{ill|Baekje|ja|百済|12=draft|12=draft}}. The following year, they returned with {{ill|Wani|ja|王仁|12=draft|12=draft}}. "Kannagiwake" is identified with Kagawake{{Sfn|Kagawake (Ancient Clan)|2010}}. This record also describes Aratawake and Kannagiwake as "ancestors of the Kamitsuke Clan." * According to the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀|12=draft|12=draft}}, "Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi" section, "Kagawake no Ō," a fifth-generation descendant of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇|12=draft|12=draft}}, was appointed as the Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko under {{ill|Emperor Seimu|ja|成務天皇|12=draft|12=draft}}'s reign.
The name Kagawake also appears in the {{ill|Sumiyoshi Taisha Jindaiki|ja|住吉大社神代記|1=Sumiyoshi Taisha Shrine Chronicles|12=draft|12=draft}} in the context of the Silla expedition.
== Analysis ==
Kagawake's existence is debated among scholars. Some argue he was a historical figure based on the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|12=draft|12=draft}}, genealogical records, and references in the {{ill|Baekje Annals|ja|百済記|12=draft|12=draft}}. Others suggest he is a later legendary addition{{Sfn|Kagawake (Ancient Clan)|2010}}.
The phonetic similarity of "Kaga" has led to theories linking Kagawake to {{ill|Ashikaga District|ja|足利郡|12=draft|12=draft}}, present-day {{ill|Ashikaga|ja|足利市|12=draft|12=draft}}, Tochigi Prefecture. Some traditions suggest Kagawake is connected to the origin of the place name "Ashikaga." <ref>{{Cite book|title=Japanese Geographic Resources|chapter=Research on Keno Province in the Kofun Period}}</ref>
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== References == * {{Cite book|title=Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Clans: Popular Edition|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館|12=draft|12=draft}}|year=2010|chapter=Kagawake|isbn=978-4642014588|ref={{Harvid|Kagawake (Ancient Clan)|2010}}}}
== See Also == * {{ill|List of pre-Asuka period figures|ja|飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Kamitsuke Clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|12=draft|12=draft}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Kogabikikami]] [[Category:Figures in Japanese mythology]] [[Category:Kofun period figures]] [[Category:Ancient Japanese figures]] [[Category:Mutsu Province people]] [[Category:Fukushima history]] [[Category:Kuni no Miyatsuko]] [[Category:Kamitsuke Clan]] [[Category:Ancient Japan–Korea relations]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:return the see also]] }} [[sn:Kagawake]] [[sn:Kogabikikami]] [[ja:鹿我別]] [[en:Kagawake]]

This record also describes Aratawake and Kannagiwake as "ancestors of the Kamitsuke Clan." * According to the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀|12=draft|12=draft}}, "Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi" section, "Kagawake no Ō," a fifth-generation descendant of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇|12=draft|12=draft}}, was appointed as the Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko under {{ill|Emperor Seimu|ja|成務天皇|12=draft|12=draft}}'s reign.
The name Kagawake also appears in the {{ill|Sumiyoshi Taisha Jindaiki|ja|住吉大社神代記|1=Sumiyoshi Taisha Shrine Chronicles|12=draft|12=draft}} in the context of the Silla expedition.
== Analysis ==
Kagawake's existence is debated among scholars. Some argue he was a historical figure based on the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|12=draft|12=draft}}, genealogical records, and references in the {{ill|Baekje Annals|ja|百済記|12=draft|12=draft}}. Others suggest he is a later legendary addition{{Sfn|Kagawake (Ancient Clan)|2010}}.
The phonetic similarity of "Kaga" has led to theories linking Kagawake to {{ill|Ashikaga District|ja|足利郡|12=draft|12=draft}}, present-day {{ill|Ashikaga|ja|足利市|12=draft|12=draft}}, Tochigi Prefecture. Some traditions suggest Kagawake is connected to the origin of the place name "Ashikaga." <ref>{{Cite book|title=Japanese Geographic Resources|chapter=Research on Keno Province in the Kofun Period}}</ref>
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== References == * {{Cite book|title=Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Clans: Popular Edition|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館|12=draft|12=draft}}|year=2010|chapter=Kagawake|isbn=978-4642014588|ref={{Harvid|Kagawake (Ancient Clan)|2010}}}}
== See Also == * {{ill|List of pre-Asuka period figures|ja|飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Kamitsuke Clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|12=draft|12=draft}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Kogabikikami]] [[Category:Figures in Japanese mythology]] [[Category:Kofun period figures]] [[Category:Ancient Japanese figures]] [[Category:Mutsu Province people]] [[Category:Fukushima history]] [[Category:Kuni no miyatsuko]] [[Category:Kamitsuke Clan]] [[Category:Ancient Japan–Korea relations]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Kagawake]] [[sn:Kogabikikami]] [[ja:鹿我別]] [[en:Kagawake]]

{{Infobox Ancient Aristocrat | Name = Kagawake | image = | image_Size = | image_Caption = | Era = [[Kofun period]] | Birth_Date = Unknown | Death_Date = Unknown | Alternate_Names = Kagawake no Mikoto, Kannagiwake, Kannagiwake no Mikoto, Kagawake no Ō, Kagawake no Mikoto | Posthumous_Name = | Deity_Name = | Dharma_Name = | Burial_Place = | Titles = [[Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko|First Ukita Kuni no Miyatsuko]] | Allegiances = [[Emperor Seimu]], [[Empress Jingū]], [[Emperor Ōjin]] | Clan = {{ill|Kamitsuke Clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Shimosuke Clan|ja|下毛野氏|1=Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno clan|12=draft|12=draft}}, Ancestor of the {{ill|Kibiya Clan|ja|吉弥侯部|12=draft|12=draft}} | Parents = | Spouse = | Children = | Notable_Features = }} '''Kagawake''' (かがわけ{{Sfn|Kagawake (Ancient Clan)|2010}}, birth and death dates unknown) is a historical figure from the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft|12=draft}} referenced in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|12=draft|12=draft}} and other historical texts. He was the first '''{{ill|Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|浮田国造|12=draft|12=draft}}''', an ancestor of the {{ill|Kamitsuke Clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Kibiya Clan|ja|吉弥侯部|12=draft|12=draft}}. Kagawake is described as a general sent to the Korean Peninsula under {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后|12=draft|12=draft}}'s reign. The "Kannagiwake" (巫別) mentioned during the {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇|12=draft|12=draft}} period is believed to be the same individual{{Sfn|Kagawake (Ancient Clan)|2010}}.
== Records ==
The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|12=draft|12=draft}} records Kagawake's involvement in the following events: * In the 49th year of {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后|12=draft|12=draft}}, he was appointed a general alongside {{ill|Aratawake|ja|荒田別|1=Aratawake|12=draft|12=draft}} to subjugate {{ill|Silla|ja|新羅|12=draft|12=draft}}. His return to Japan is noted in the 50th year. * In the 15th year of {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇|12=draft|12=draft}}, "Kannagiwake" was dispatched with Aratawake to {{ill|Baekje|ja|百済|12=draft|12=draft}}. The following year, they returned with {{ill|Wani|ja|王仁|12=draft|12=draft}}. "Kannagiwake" is identified with Kagawake{{Sfn|Kagawake (Ancient Clan)|2010}}. This record also describes Aratawake and Kannagiwake as "ancestors of the Kamitsuke Clan." * According to the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀|12=draft|12=draft}}, "Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi" section, "Kagawake no Ō," a fifth-generation descendant of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇|12=draft|12=draft}}, was appointed as the Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko under {{ill|Emperor Seimu|ja|成務天皇|12=draft|12=draft}}'s reign.
The name Kagawake also appears in the {{ill|Sumiyoshi Taisha Jindaiki|ja|住吉大社神代記|1=Sumiyoshi Taisha Shrine Chronicles|12=draft|12=draft}} in the context of the Silla expedition.
== Analysis ==
Kagawake's existence is debated among scholars. Some argue he was a historical figure based on the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|12=draft|12=draft}}, genealogical records, and references in the {{ill|Baekje Annals|ja|百済記|12=draft|12=draft}}. Others suggest he is a later legendary addition{{Sfn|Kagawake (Ancient Clan)|2010}}.
The phonetic similarity of "Kaga" has led to theories linking Kagawake to {{ill|Ashikaga District|ja|足利郡|12=draft|12=draft}}, present-day {{ill|Ashikaga|ja|足利市|12=draft|12=draft}}, Tochigi Prefecture. Some traditions suggest Kagawake is connected to the origin of the place name "Ashikaga." <ref>{{Cite book|title=Japanese Geographic Resources|chapter=Research on Keno Province in the Kofun Period}}</ref>
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== References == * {{Cite book|title=Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Clans: Popular Edition|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館|12=draft|12=draft}}|year=2010|chapter=Kagawake|isbn=978-4642014588|ref={{Harvid|Kagawake (Ancient Clan)|2010}}}}
== See Also == * {{ill|List of pre-Asuka period figures|ja|飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Kamitsuke Clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|12=draft|12=draft}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]]

22 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:鹿我別 up to rev 99874817
revision.text:
{{基礎情報 公家 | 氏名 = 鹿我別 | 画像 = | 画像サイズ = | 画像説明 = | 時代 = [[古墳時代]] | 生誕 = 不明 | 死没 = 不明 | 改名 = | 別名 = 鹿我別命、巫別、巫別命、賀我別命、賀我別王 | 諡号 = | 神号 = | 戒名 = | 墓所 = | 官位 = [[浮田国造]] | 主君 = [[成務天皇]]、[[神功皇后]]、[[応神天皇]] | 氏族 = [[上毛野氏|上毛野君]]・[[下毛野氏|下毛野君]]同族、[[吉弥侯部]]祖 | 父母 = | 妻 = | 子 = | 特記事項 = }} '''鹿我別'''（かがわけ{{Sfn|鹿我別（古代氏族）|2010年}}、[[生没年不詳]]）は、『[[日本書紀]]』等に伝わる[[古墳時代]]の[[豪族]]で初代'''[[浮田国造]]'''。[[上毛野氏|上毛野君]]・[[吉弥侯部]]の遠祖で、[[神功皇后]]の時に[[朝鮮]]へ派遣されたという[[武将]]である。[[応神天皇]]年間に派遣された「'''巫別'''（かんなぎわけ、かがわけ）」は同一人物とされる{{Sfn|鹿我別（古代氏族）|2010年}}。'''鹿我別命'''、'''賀我別王'''とも。
== 記録 == 『[[日本書紀]]』[[神功皇后]]49年3月条によると、[[荒田別]]とともに将軍に任じられ[[新羅]]征討に参加した。神功皇后50年2月条、帰国した旨が記される。
同書[[応神天皇]]15年8月条では、巫別が荒田別とともに[[百済]]に派遣され、翌年[[王仁]]を連れて帰っている。この「巫別」は鹿我別と同一人物とされる{{Sfn|鹿我別（古代氏族）|2010年}}。この条において、荒田別・巫別は「上毛野君の祖」と記載されている。
『[[先代旧事本紀]]』「[[国造本紀]]」[[浮田国造]]条では、[[成務天皇]]年間に崇神天皇五世孫の「賀我別王」が国造に任じられたと記載されている。
なお鹿我別の名は、『[[住吉大社神代記]]』でも新羅征討の説話において記載されている。
== 考証 == 鹿我別の存在については、『日本書紀』や各種系図、『[[百済記]]』を原資料とする実在の人物であるのか、後世に追記された伝説上の人物であるか、説が分かれている{{Sfn|鹿我別（古代氏族）|2010年}}。
「かが」の読みから、[[下野国]][[足利郡]]（現・[[栃木県]][[足利市]]周辺）と関連づける説がある<ref>『日本地理志料』に記載（『毛野国の研究 古墳時代の解明 下』第三章四節より）。また、鹿我別は地名「足利」の由来の一説とされている（[https://crd.ndl.go.jp/reference/modules/d3ndlcrdentry/index.php?page=ref_view&id=1000047466 足利の地名の由来を知りたい。（栃木県立足利図書館回答）]（レファレンス協同データベース））。</ref>。
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}}
== 参考文献 == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=鹿我別|title=日本古代氏族人名辞典 普及版|publisher=[[吉川弘文館]]|isbn=978-4642014588|ref={{Harvid|鹿我別（古代氏族）|2010年}}}}
== 関連項目 == * [[飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧]] * [[上毛野氏]]

== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}}
== 参考文献 == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=鹿我別|title=日本古代氏族人名辞典 普及版|publisher=[[吉川弘文館]]|isbn=978-4642014588|ref={{Harvid|鹿我別（古代氏族）|2010年}}}}

He was the first '''{{ill|Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|浮田国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}''', an ancestor of the {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|qq=}} and {{ill|Kibiya clan|ja|吉弥侯部|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibiya Clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 吉美侯部}}. Kagawake is described as a general sent to the Korean Peninsula under [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]]'s reign. The "Kannagiwake" (巫別) mentioned during the [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]] period is believed to be the same individual{{Sfn|Kagawake (Ancient Clan)|2010}}.
== Records ==
The [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] records Kagawake's involvement in the following events: * In the 49th year of [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]], he was appointed a general alongside {{ill|Aratawake|ja|荒田別|1=Aratawake|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aratawake|13=User:Immanuelle/Aratawake|qq=}} to subjugate [[:en:Silla|Silla]]. His return to Japan is noted in the 50th year. * In the 15th year of [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]], "Kannagiwake" was dispatched with Aratawake to [[:en:Baekje|Baekje]]. The following year, they returned with [[:en:Wani (scholar)|Wani]]. "Kannagiwake" is identified with Kagawake{{Sfn|Kagawake (Ancient Clan)|2010}}. This record also describes Aratawake and Kannagiwake as "ancestors of the Kamitsukeno clan." * According to the [[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]], "Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi" section, "Kagawake no Ō," a fifth-generation descendant of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]], was appointed as the Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko under [[:en:Emperor Seimu|Emperor Seimu]]'s reign.
The name Kagawake also appears in the {{ill|Sumiyoshi Taisha Jindaiki|ja|住吉大社神代記|1=Sumiyoshi Taisha Shrine Chronicles|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi Taisha Jindaiki|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi Taisha Shrine Chronicles|qq=}} in the context of the Silla expedition.
== Analysis ==
Kagawake's existence is debated among scholars. Some argue he was a historical figure based on the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], genealogical records, and references in the {{ill|Baekje Annals|ja|百済記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baekje Annals|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 百済三書}}. Others suggest he is a later legendary addition{{Sfn|Kagawake (Ancient Clan)|2010}}.
The phonetic similarity of "Kaga" has led to theories linking Kagawake to [[:en:Ashikaga District, Tochigi|Ashikaga District]], present-day [[:en:Ashikaga, Tochigi|Ashikaga]], Tochigi Prefecture. Some traditions suggest Kagawake is connected to the origin of the place name "Ashikaga." <ref>{{Cite book|title=Japanese Geographic Resources|chapter=Research on Keno Province in the Kofun Period}}</ref>

revision.comment: Text replacement - "qid=" to "WD="
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:鹿我別]] {{Infobox person|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|father={{ill|Q17230461|WD=Q17230461}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q17226573]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:15, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q17226573]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:54, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox Ancient Aristocrat | Name = Kagawake | image = | image_Size = | image_Caption = | Era = [[Kofun period]] | Birth_Date = Unknown | Death_Date = Unknown | Alternate_Names = Kagawake no Mikoto, Kannagiwake, Kannagiwake no Mikoto, Kagawake no Ō, Kagawake no Mikoto | Posthumous_Name = | Deity_Name = | Dharma_Name = | Burial_Place = | Titles = [[Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko|First Ukita Kuni no Miyatsuko]] | Allegiances = [[Emperor Seimu]], [[Empress Jingū]], [[Emperor Ōjin]] | Clan = {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|qq=}}, {{ill|Shimosuke Clan|ja|下毛野氏|1=Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno clan|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimosuke Clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno clan|qq=}}, Ancestor of the {{ill|Kibiya clan|ja|吉弥侯部|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibiya Clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 吉美侯部}} | Parents = | Spouse = | Children = | Notable_Features = }} '''Kagawake''' (かがわけ{{Sfn|Kagawake (Ancient Clan)|2010}}, birth and death dates unknown) is a historical figure from the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] referenced in the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] and other historical texts. He was the first '''{{ill|Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|浮田国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}''', an ancestor of the {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|qq=}} and {{ill|Kibiya clan|ja|吉弥侯部|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibiya Clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 吉美侯部}}. Kagawake is described as a general sent to the Korean Peninsula under [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]]'s reign. The "Kannagiwake" (巫別) mentioned during the [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]] period is believed to be the same individual{{Sfn|Kagawake (Ancient Clan)|2010}}.
== Records ==
The [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] records Kagawake's involvement in the following events: * In the 49th year of [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]], he was appointed a general alongside {{ill|Aratawake|ja|荒田別|1=Aratawake|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aratawake|13=User:Immanuelle/Aratawake|qq=}} to subjugate [[:en:Silla|Silla]]. His return to Japan is noted in the 50th year. * In the 15th year of [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]], "Kannagiwake" was dispatched with Aratawake to [[:en:Baekje|Baekje]]. The following year, they returned with [[:en:Wani (scholar)|Wani]]. "Kannagiwake" is identified with Kagawake{{Sfn|Kagawake (Ancient Clan)|2010}}. This record also describes Aratawake and Kannagiwake as "ancestors of the Kamitsukeno clan." * According to the [[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]], "Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi" section, "Kagawake no Ō," a fifth-generation descendant of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]], was appointed as the Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko under [[:en:Emperor Seimu|Emperor Seimu]]'s reign.
The name Kagawake also appears in the {{ill|Sumiyoshi Taisha Jindaiki|ja|住吉大社神代記|1=Sumiyoshi Taisha Shrine Chronicles|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi Taisha Jindaiki|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi Taisha Shrine Chronicles|qq=}} in the context of the Silla expedition.
== Analysis ==
Kagawake's existence is debated among scholars. Some argue he was a historical figure based on the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], genealogical records, and references in the {{ill|Baekje Annals|ja|百済記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baekje Annals|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 百済三書}}. Others suggest he is a later legendary addition{{Sfn|Kagawake (Ancient Clan)|2010}}.
The phonetic similarity of "Kaga" has led to theories linking Kagawake to [[:en:Ashikaga District, Tochigi|Ashikaga District]], present-day [[:en:Ashikaga, Tochigi|Ashikaga]], Tochigi Prefecture. Some traditions suggest Kagawake is connected to the origin of the place name "Ashikaga." <ref>{{Cite book|title=Japanese Geographic Resources|chapter=Research on Keno Province in the Kofun Period}}</ref>

He was the first '''{{ill|Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|浮田国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}''', an ancestor of the {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|qq=}} and {{ill|Kibiya clan|ja|吉弥侯部|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibiya Clan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 吉美侯部}}. Kagawake is described as a general sent to the Korean Peninsula under [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]]'s reign. The "Kannagiwake" (巫別) mentioned during the [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]] period is believed to be the same individual{{Sfn|Kagawake (Ancient Clan)|2010}}.
== Records ==
The [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] records Kagawake's involvement in the following events: * In the 49th year of [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]], he was appointed a general alongside {{ill|Aratawake|ja|荒田別|1=Aratawake|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aratawake|13=User:Immanuelle/Aratawake|qq=}} to subjugate [[:en:Silla|Silla]]. His return to Japan is noted in the 50th year. * In the 15th year of [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]], "Kannagiwake" was dispatched with Aratawake to [[:en:Baekje|Baekje]]. The following year, they returned with [[:en:Wani (scholar)|Wani]]. "Kannagiwake" is identified with Kagawake{{Sfn|Kagawake (Ancient Clan)|2010}}. This record also describes Aratawake and Kannagiwake as "ancestors of the Kamitsukeno clan." * According to the [[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]], "Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi" section, "Kagawake no Ō," a fifth-generation descendant of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]], was appointed as the Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko under [[:en:Emperor Seimu|Emperor Seimu]]'s reign.
The name Kagawake also appears in the {{ill|Sumiyoshi Taisha Jindaiki|ja|住吉大社神代記|1=Sumiyoshi Taisha Shrine Chronicles|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi Taisha Jindaiki|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi Taisha Shrine Chronicles|qq=}} in the context of the Silla expedition.
== Analysis ==
Kagawake's existence is debated among scholars. Some argue he was a historical figure based on the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], genealogical records, and references in the {{ill|Baekje Annals|ja|百済記|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baekje Annals|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 百済三書}}. Others suggest he is a later legendary addition{{Sfn|Kagawake (Ancient Clan)|2010}}.
The phonetic similarity of "Kaga" has led to theories linking Kagawake to [[:en:Ashikaga District, Tochigi|Ashikaga District]], present-day [[:en:Ashikaga, Tochigi|Ashikaga]], Tochigi Prefecture. Some traditions suggest Kagawake is connected to the origin of the place name "Ashikaga." <ref>{{Cite book|title=Japanese Geographic Resources|chapter=Research on Keno Province in the Kofun Period}}</ref>
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== References == * {{Cite book|title=Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Clans: Popular Edition|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|1=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4346755}}|year=2010|chapter=Kagawake|isbn=978-4642014588|ref={{Harvid|Kagawake (Ancient Clan)|2010}}}}
== See Also == * {{ill|List of pre-Asuka period figures|ja|飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of pre-Asuka period figures|1=List of ancient Japanese figures|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11665973}} * {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|qq=}}
{{translated page|ja|鹿我別|version=99874817|comment=Imported full ja history}}

He was the first '''{{ill|Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|浮田国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}''', an ancestor of the {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|qq=}} and {{ill|Kibiya clan|ja|吉弥侯部|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibiya Clan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 吉美侯部}}. Kagawake is described as a general sent to the Korean Peninsula under [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]]'s reign. The "Kannagiwake" (巫別) mentioned during the [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]] period is believed to be the same individual{{Sfn|Kagawake (Ancient Clan)|2010}}.
== Records ==
The [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] records Kagawake's involvement in the following events: * In the 49th year of [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]], he was appointed a general alongside {{ill|Aratawake|ja|荒田別|1=Aratawake|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aratawake|13=User:Immanuelle/Aratawake|qq=}} to subjugate [[:en:Silla|Silla]]. His return to Japan is noted in the 50th year. * In the 15th year of [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]], "Kannagiwake" was dispatched with Aratawake to [[:en:Baekje|Baekje]]. The following year, they returned with [[:en:Wani (scholar)|Wani]]. "Kannagiwake" is identified with Kagawake{{Sfn|Kagawake (Ancient Clan)|2010}}. This record also describes Aratawake and Kannagiwake as "ancestors of the Kamitsukeno clan." * According to the [[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]], "Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi" section, "Kagawake no Ō," a fifth-generation descendant of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]], was appointed as the Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko under [[:en:Emperor Seimu|Emperor Seimu]]'s reign.
The name Kagawake also appears in the {{ill|Sumiyoshi Taisha Jindaiki|ja|住吉大社神代記|1=Sumiyoshi Taisha Shrine Chronicles|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi Taisha Jindaiki|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi Taisha Shrine Chronicles|qq=}} in the context of the Silla expedition.
== Analysis ==
Kagawake's existence is debated among scholars. Some argue he was a historical figure based on the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], genealogical records, and references in the {{ill|Baekje Annals|ja|百済記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baekje Annals|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 百済三書}}. Others suggest he is a later legendary addition{{Sfn|Kagawake (Ancient Clan)|2010}}.
The phonetic similarity of "Kaga" has led to theories linking Kagawake to [[:en:Ashikaga District, Tochigi|Ashikaga District]], present-day [[:en:Ashikaga, Tochigi|Ashikaga]], Tochigi Prefecture. Some traditions suggest Kagawake is connected to the origin of the place name "Ashikaga." <ref>{{Cite book|title=Japanese Geographic Resources|chapter=Research on Keno Province in the Kofun Period}}</ref>

The following year, they returned with [[:en:Wani (scholar)|Wani]]. "Kannagiwake" is identified with Kagawake{{Sfn|Kagawake (Ancient Clan)|2010}}. This record also describes Aratawake and Kannagiwake as "ancestors of the Kamitsukeno clan." * According to the [[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]], "Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi" section, "Kagawake no Ō," a fifth-generation descendant of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]], was appointed as the Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko under [[:en:Emperor Seimu|Emperor Seimu]]'s reign.
The name Kagawake also appears in the {{ill|Sumiyoshi Taisha Jindaiki|ja|住吉大社神代記|1=Sumiyoshi Taisha Shrine Chronicles|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi Taisha Jindaiki|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi Taisha Shrine Chronicles|qq=}} in the context of the Silla expedition.
== Analysis ==
Kagawake's existence is debated among scholars. Some argue he was a historical figure based on the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], genealogical records, and references in the {{ill|Baekje Annals|ja|百済記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baekje Annals|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 百済三書}}. Others suggest he is a later legendary addition{{Sfn|Kagawake (Ancient Clan)|2010}}.
The phonetic similarity of "Kaga" has led to theories linking Kagawake to [[:en:Ashikaga District, Tochigi|Ashikaga District]], present-day [[:en:Ashikaga, Tochigi|Ashikaga]], Tochigi Prefecture. Some traditions suggest Kagawake is connected to the origin of the place name "Ashikaga." <ref>{{Cite book|title=Japanese Geographic Resources|chapter=Research on Keno Province in the Kofun Period}}</ref>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:鹿我別]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:鹿我別]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:鹿我別]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:鹿我別]] {{Infobox person|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|father={{ill|Q17230461|WD=Q17230461}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q17226573]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:15, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q17226573]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:54, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox Ancient Aristocrat | Name = Kagawake | image = | image_Size = | image_Caption = | Era = [[Kofun period]] | Birth_Date = Unknown | Death_Date = Unknown | Alternate_Names = Kagawake no Mikoto, Kannagiwake, Kannagiwake no Mikoto, Kagawake no Ō, Kagawake no Mikoto | Posthumous_Name = | Deity_Name = | Dharma_Name = | Burial_Place = | Titles = [[Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko|First Ukita Kuni no Miyatsuko]] | Allegiances = [[Emperor Seimu]], [[Empress Jingū]], [[Emperor Ōjin]] | Clan = {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|qq=}}, {{ill|Shimosuke Clan|ja|下毛野氏|1=Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimosuke Clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno clan|qq=}}, Ancestor of the {{ill|Kibiya clan|ja|吉弥侯部|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibiya Clan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 吉美侯部}} | Parents = | Spouse = | Children = | Notable_Features = }} '''Kagawake''' (かがわけ{{Sfn|Kagawake (Ancient Clan)|2010}}, birth and death dates unknown) is a historical figure from the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] referenced in the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] and other historical texts. He was the first '''{{ill|Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|浮田国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}''', an ancestor of the {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|qq=}} and {{ill|Kibiya clan|ja|吉弥侯部|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibiya Clan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 吉美侯部}}. Kagawake is described as a general sent to the Korean Peninsula under [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]]'s reign. The "Kannagiwake" (巫別) mentioned during the [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]] period is believed to be the same individual{{Sfn|Kagawake (Ancient Clan)|2010}}.
== Records ==
The [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] records Kagawake's involvement in the following events: * In the 49th year of [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]], he was appointed a general alongside {{ill|Aratawake|ja|荒田別|1=Aratawake|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aratawake|13=User:Immanuelle/Aratawake|qq=}} to subjugate [[:en:Silla|Silla]]. His return to Japan is noted in the 50th year. * In the 15th year of [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]], "Kannagiwake" was dispatched with Aratawake to [[:en:Baekje|Baekje]]. The following year, they returned with [[:en:Wani (scholar)|Wani]]. "Kannagiwake" is identified with Kagawake{{Sfn|Kagawake (Ancient Clan)|2010}}. This record also describes Aratawake and Kannagiwake as "ancestors of the Kamitsukeno clan." * According to the [[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]], "Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi" section, "Kagawake no Ō," a fifth-generation descendant of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]], was appointed as the Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko under [[:en:Emperor Seimu|Emperor Seimu]]'s reign.
The name Kagawake also appears in the {{ill|Sumiyoshi Taisha Jindaiki|ja|住吉大社神代記|1=Sumiyoshi Taisha Shrine Chronicles|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi Taisha Jindaiki|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi Taisha Shrine Chronicles|qq=}} in the context of the Silla expedition.
== Analysis ==
Kagawake's existence is debated among scholars. Some argue he was a historical figure based on the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], genealogical records, and references in the {{ill|Baekje Annals|ja|百済記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baekje Annals|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 百済三書}}. Others suggest he is a later legendary addition{{Sfn|Kagawake (Ancient Clan)|2010}}.
The phonetic similarity of "Kaga" has led to theories linking Kagawake to [[:en:Ashikaga District, Tochigi|Ashikaga District]], present-day [[:en:Ashikaga, Tochigi|Ashikaga]], Tochigi Prefecture. Some traditions suggest Kagawake is connected to the origin of the place name "Ashikaga." <ref>{{Cite book|title=Japanese Geographic Resources|chapter=Research on Keno Province in the Kofun Period}}</ref>
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== References == * {{Cite book|title=Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Clans: Popular Edition|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|1=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4346755}}|year=2010|chapter=Kagawake|isbn=978-4642014588|ref={{Harvid|Kagawake (Ancient Clan)|2010}}}}
== See Also == * {{ill|List of pre-Asuka period figures|ja|飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of pre-Asuka period figures|1=List of ancient Japanese figures|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11665973}} * {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|qq=}}
{{translated page|ja|鹿我別|version=99874817|comment=Imported full ja history}}

Some argue he was a historical figure based on the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], genealogical records, and references in the {{ill|Baekje Annals|ja|百済記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baekje Annals|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 百済三書}}. Others suggest he is a later legendary addition{{Sfn|Kagawake (Ancient Clan)|2010}}.
The phonetic similarity of "Kaga" has led to theories linking Kagawake to [[:en:Ashikaga District, Tochigi|Ashikaga District]], present-day [[:en:Ashikaga, Tochigi|Ashikaga]], Tochigi Prefecture. Some traditions suggest Kagawake is connected to the origin of the place name "Ashikaga." <ref>{{Cite book|title=Japanese Geographic Resources|chapter=Research on Keno Province in the Kofun Period}}</ref>

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:鹿我別]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:鹿我別]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:鹿我別]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:鹿我別]] {{Infobox person|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|father={{ill|Q17230461|WD=Q17230461}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q17226573]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:15, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q17226573]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:54, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox Ancient Aristocrat | Name = Kagawake | image = | image_Size = | image_Caption = | Era = [[Kofun period]] | Birth_Date = Unknown | Death_Date = Unknown | Alternate_Names = Kagawake no Mikoto, Kannagiwake, Kannagiwake no Mikoto, Kagawake no Ō, Kagawake no Mikoto | Posthumous_Name = | Deity_Name = | Dharma_Name = | Burial_Place = | Titles = [[Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko|First Ukita Kuni no Miyatsuko]] | Allegiances = [[Emperor Seimu]], [[Empress Jingū]], [[Emperor Ōjin]] | Clan = {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|qq=}}, {{ill|Shimosuke Clan|ja|下毛野氏|1=Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimosuke Clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno clan|qq=}}, Ancestor of the {{ill|Kibiya clan|ja|吉弥侯部|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibiya Clan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 吉美侯部}} | Parents = | Spouse = | Children = | Notable_Features = }} '''Kagawake''' (かがわけ{{Sfn|Kagawake (Ancient Clan)|2010}}, birth and death dates unknown) is a historical figure from the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] referenced in the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] and other historical texts. He was the first '''{{ill|Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|浮田国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}''', an ancestor of the {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|qq=}} and {{ill|Kibiya clan|ja|吉弥侯部|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibiya Clan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 吉美侯部}}. Kagawake is described as a general sent to the Korean Peninsula under [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]]'s reign. The "Kannagiwake" (巫別) mentioned during the [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]] period is believed to be the same individual{{Sfn|Kagawake (Ancient Clan)|2010}}.
== Records ==
The [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] records Kagawake's involvement in the following events: * In the 49th year of [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]], he was appointed a general alongside {{ill|Aratawake|ja|荒田別|1=Aratawake|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aratawake|13=User:Immanuelle/Aratawake|qq=}} to subjugate [[:en:Silla|Silla]]. His return to Japan is noted in the 50th year. * In the 15th year of [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]], "Kannagiwake" was dispatched with Aratawake to [[:en:Baekje|Baekje]]. The following year, they returned with [[:en:Wani (scholar)|Wani]]. "Kannagiwake" is identified with Kagawake{{Sfn|Kagawake (Ancient Clan)|2010}}. This record also describes Aratawake and Kannagiwake as "ancestors of the Kamitsukeno clan." * According to the [[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]], "Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi" section, "Kagawake no Ō," a fifth-generation descendant of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]], was appointed as the Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko under [[:en:Emperor Seimu|Emperor Seimu]]'s reign.
The name Kagawake also appears in the {{ill|Sumiyoshi Taisha Jindaiki|ja|住吉大社神代記|1=Sumiyoshi Taisha Shrine Chronicles|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi Taisha Jindaiki|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi Taisha Shrine Chronicles|qq=}} in the context of the Silla expedition.
== Analysis ==
Kagawake's existence is debated among scholars. Some argue he was a historical figure based on the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], genealogical records, and references in the {{ill|Baekje Annals|ja|百済記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baekje Annals|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 百済三書}}. Others suggest he is a later legendary addition{{Sfn|Kagawake (Ancient Clan)|2010}}.
The phonetic similarity of "Kaga" has led to theories linking Kagawake to [[:en:Ashikaga District, Tochigi|Ashikaga District]], present-day [[:en:Ashikaga, Tochigi|Ashikaga]], Tochigi Prefecture. Some traditions suggest Kagawake is connected to the origin of the place name "Ashikaga." <ref>{{Cite book|title=Japanese Geographic Resources|chapter=Research on Keno Province in the Kofun Period}}</ref>
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== References == * {{Cite book|title=Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Clans: Popular Edition|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|1=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4346755}}|year=2010|chapter=Kagawake|isbn=978-4642014588|ref={{Harvid|Kagawake (Ancient Clan)|2010}}}}
