{{Infobox Ancient Aristocrat | Name = Kagawake | image = | image_Size = | image_Caption = | Era = [[Kofun period]] | Birth_Date = Unknown | Death_Date = Unknown | Alternate_Names = Kagawake no Mikoto, Kannagiwake, Kannagiwake no Mikoto, Kagawake no Ō, Kagawake no Mikoto | Posthumous_Name = | Deity_Name = | Dharma_Name = | Burial_Place = | Titles = [[Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko|First Ukita Kuni no Miyatsuko]] | Allegiances = [[Emperor Seimu]], [[Empress Jingū]], [[Emperor Ōjin]] | Clan = {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|qq=}}, {{ill|Shimosuke Clan|ja|下毛野氏|1=Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimosuke Clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno clan|qq=}}, Ancestor of the {{ill|Kibiya clan|ja|吉弥侯部|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibiya Clan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 吉美侯部}} | Parents = | Spouse = | Children = | Notable_Features = }} '''Kagawake''' (かがわけ{{Sfn|Kagawake (Ancient Clan)|2010}}, birth and death dates unknown) is a historical figure from the {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period}} referenced in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki}} and other historical texts. He was the first '''{{ill|Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|浮田国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}''', an ancestor of the {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|qq=}} and {{ill|Kibiya clan|ja|吉弥侯部|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibiya Clan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 吉美侯部}}. Kagawake is described as a general sent to the Korean Peninsula under {{ill|Empress Jingū|en|Empress Jingū|WD=Q232803|lt=Empress Jingū}}'s reign. The "Kannagiwake" (巫別) mentioned during the {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Emperor Ōjin}} period is believed to be the same individual{{Sfn|Kagawake (Ancient Clan)|2010}}.
== Records ==
The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki}} records Kagawake's involvement in the following events: * In the 49th year of {{ill|Empress Jingū|en|Empress Jingū|WD=Q232803|lt=Empress Jingū}}, he was appointed a general alongside {{ill|Aratawake|ja|荒田別|1=Aratawake|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aratawake|13=User:Immanuelle/Aratawake|qq=}} to subjugate {{ill|Silla|en|Silla|WD=Q28456|lt=Silla}}. His return to Japan is noted in the 50th year. * In the 15th year of {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Emperor Ōjin}}, "Kannagiwake" was dispatched with Aratawake to [[:en:Baekje|Baekje]]. The following year, they returned with {{ill|Wani (scholar)|en|Wani (scholar)|WD=Q482722|lt=Wani}}. "Kannagiwake" is identified with Kagawake{{Sfn|Kagawake (Ancient Clan)|2010}}. This record also describes Aratawake and Kannagiwake as "ancestors of the Kamitsukeno clan." * According to the {{ill|Kujiki|en|Kujiki|WD=Q2716751|lt=Sendai Kuji Hongi}}, "Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi" section, "Kagawake no Ō," a fifth-generation descendant of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|en|Emperor Sujin|WD=Q333530|lt=Emperor Sujin}}, was appointed as the Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko under {{ill|Emperor Seimu|en|Emperor Seimu|WD=Q316672|lt=Emperor Seimu}}'s reign.
The name Kagawake also appears in the {{ill|Sumiyoshi Taisha Jindaiki|ja|住吉大社神代記|1=Sumiyoshi Taisha Shrine Chronicles|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi Taisha Jindaiki|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi Taisha Shrine Chronicles|qq=}} in the context of the Silla expedition.
== Analysis ==
Kagawake's existence is debated among scholars. Some argue he was a historical figure based on the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki}}, genealogical records, and references in the {{ill|Baekje Annals|ja|百済記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baekje Annals|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 百済三書}}. Others suggest he is a later legendary addition{{Sfn|Kagawake (Ancient Clan)|2010}}.
The phonetic similarity of "Kaga" has led to theories linking Kagawake to {{ill|Ashikaga District, Tochigi|en|Ashikaga District, Tochigi|WD=Q11636831|lt=Ashikaga District}}, present-day {{ill|Ashikaga, Tochigi|en|Ashikaga, Tochigi|WD=Q374477|lt=Ashikaga}}, Tochigi Prefecture. Some traditions suggest Kagawake is connected to the origin of the place name "Ashikaga." <ref>{{Cite book|title=Japanese Geographic Resources|chapter=Research on Keno Province in the Kofun Period}}</ref>
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== References == * {{Cite book|title=Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Clans: Popular Edition|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|1=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4346755}}|year=2010|chapter=Kagawake|isbn=978-4642014588|ref={{Harvid|Kagawake (Ancient Clan)|2010}}}}
== See Also == * {{ill|List of pre-Asuka period figures|ja|飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of pre-Asuka period figures|1=List of ancient Japanese figures|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11665973}} * {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|qq=}}
{{translated page|ja|鹿我別|version=99874817|comment=Imported full ja history}}

The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki}} records Kagawake's involvement in the following events: * In the 49th year of {{ill|Empress Jingū|en|Empress Jingū|WD=Q232803|lt=Empress Jingū}}, he was appointed a general alongside {{ill|Aratawake|ja|荒田別|1=Aratawake|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aratawake|13=User:Immanuelle/Aratawake|qq=}} to subjugate {{ill|Silla|en|Silla|WD=Q28456|lt=Silla}}. His return to Japan is noted in the 50th year. * In the 15th year of {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Emperor Ōjin}}, "Kannagiwake" was dispatched with Aratawake to [[:en:Baekje|Baekje]]. The following year, they returned with {{ill|Wani (scholar)|en|Wani (scholar)|WD=Q482722|lt=Wani}}. "Kannagiwake" is identified with Kagawake{{Sfn|Kagawake (Ancient Clan)|2010}}. This record also describes Aratawake and Kannagiwake as "ancestors of the Kamitsukeno clan." * According to the {{ill|Kujiki|en|Kujiki|WD=Q2716751|lt=Sendai Kuji Hongi}}, "Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi" section, "Kagawake no Ō," a fifth-generation descendant of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|en|Emperor Sujin|WD=Q333530|lt=Emperor Sujin}}, was appointed as the Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko under {{ill|Emperor Seimu|en|Emperor Seimu|WD=Q316672|lt=Emperor Seimu}}'s reign.
The name Kagawake also appears in the {{ill|Sumiyoshi Taisha Jindaiki|ja|住吉大社神代記|1=Sumiyoshi Taisha Shrine Chronicles|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi Taisha Jindaiki|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi Taisha Shrine Chronicles|qq=}} in the context of the Silla expedition.
== Analysis ==
Kagawake's existence is debated among scholars. Some argue he was a historical figure based on the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki}}, genealogical records, and references in the {{ill|Baekje Annals|ja|百済記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baekje Annals|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 百済三書}}. Others suggest he is a later legendary addition{{Sfn|Kagawake (Ancient Clan)|2010}}.
The phonetic similarity of "Kaga" has led to theories linking Kagawake to {{ill|Ashikaga District, Tochigi|en|Ashikaga District, Tochigi|WD=Q11636831|lt=Ashikaga District}}, present-day {{ill|Ashikaga, Tochigi|en|Ashikaga, Tochigi|WD=Q374477|lt=Ashikaga}}, Tochigi Prefecture. Some traditions suggest Kagawake is connected to the origin of the place name "Ashikaga." <ref>{{Cite book|title=Japanese Geographic Resources|chapter=Research on Keno Province in the Kofun Period}}</ref>

The phonetic similarity of "Kaga" has led to theories linking Kagawake to {{ill|Ashikaga District, Tochigi|en|Ashikaga District, Tochigi|WD=Q11636831|lt=Ashikaga District|1=Ashikaga District, Tochigi}}, present-day {{ill|Ashikaga, Tochigi|en|Ashikaga, Tochigi|WD=Q374477|lt=Ashikaga|1=Ashikaga, Tochigi}}, Tochigi Prefecture. Some traditions suggest Kagawake is connected to the origin of the place name "Ashikaga." <ref>{{Cite book|title=Japanese Geographic Resources|chapter=Research on Keno Province in the Kofun Period}}</ref>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:鹿我別]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:鹿我別]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:鹿我別]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:鹿我別]] {{Infobox person|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097|1=Male (gender)}}|father={{ill|Q17230461|WD=Q17230461|1=Mimorowake|2=ja|3=御諸別王|lt=Mimorowake|4=zh|5=御諸別王|WD=Q17230461}}}}
{{Infobox Ancient Aristocrat | Name = Kagawake | image = | image_Size = | image_Caption = | Era = [[Kofun period]] | Birth_Date = Unknown | Death_Date = Unknown | Alternate_Names = Kagawake no Mikoto, Kannagiwake, Kannagiwake no Mikoto, Kagawake no Ō, Kagawake no Mikoto | Posthumous_Name = | Deity_Name = | Dharma_Name = | Burial_Place = | Titles = [[Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko|First Ukita Kuni no Miyatsuko]] | Allegiances = [[Emperor Seimu]], [[Empress Jingū]], [[Emperor Ōjin]] | Clan = {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|qq=}}, {{ill|Shimosuke Clan|ja|下毛野氏|1=Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimosuke Clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno clan|qq=}}, Ancestor of the {{ill|Kibiya clan|ja|吉弥侯部|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibiya Clan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 吉美侯部}} | Parents = | Spouse = | Children = | Notable_Features = }} '''Kagawake''' (かがわけ{{Sfn|Kagawake (Ancient Clan)|2010}}, birth and death dates unknown) is a historical figure from the {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period|1=Kofun period}} referenced in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki|1=Nihon Shoki}} and other historical texts. He was the first '''{{ill|Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|浮田国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}''', an ancestor of the {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|qq=}} and {{ill|Kibiya clan|ja|吉弥侯部|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibiya Clan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 吉美侯部}}. Kagawake is described as a general sent to the Korean Peninsula under {{ill|Empress Jingū|en|Empress Jingū|WD=Q232803|lt=Empress Jingū|1=Empress Jingū}}'s reign. The "Kannagiwake" (巫別) mentioned during the {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Emperor Ōjin|1=Emperor Ōjin}} period is believed to be the same individual{{Sfn|Kagawake (Ancient Clan)|2010}}.
== Records ==
The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki|1=Nihon Shoki}} records Kagawake's involvement in the following events: * In the 49th year of {{ill|Empress Jingū|en|Empress Jingū|WD=Q232803|lt=Empress Jingū|1=Empress Jingū}}, he was appointed a general alongside {{ill|Aratawake|ja|荒田別|1=Aratawake|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aratawake|13=User:Immanuelle/Aratawake|qq=}} to subjugate {{ill|Silla|en|Silla|WD=Q28456|lt=Silla|1=Silla}}. His return to Japan is noted in the 50th year. * In the 15th year of {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Emperor Ōjin|1=Emperor Ōjin}}, "Kannagiwake" was dispatched with Aratawake to [[:en:Baekje|Baekje]]. The following year, they returned with {{ill|Wani (scholar)|en|Wani (scholar)|WD=Q482722|lt=Wani|1=Wani (scholar)}}. "Kannagiwake" is identified with Kagawake{{Sfn|Kagawake (Ancient Clan)|2010}}. This record also describes Aratawake and Kannagiwake as "ancestors of the Kamitsukeno clan." * According to the {{ill|Kujiki|en|Kujiki|WD=Q2716751|lt=Sendai Kuji Hongi|1=Kujiki}}, "Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi" section, "Kagawake no Ō," a fifth-generation descendant of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|en|Emperor Sujin|WD=Q333530|lt=Emperor Sujin|1=Emperor Sujin}}, was appointed as the Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko under {{ill|Emperor Seimu|en|Emperor Seimu|WD=Q316672|lt=Emperor Seimu|1=Emperor Seimu}}'s reign.
The name Kagawake also appears in the {{ill|Sumiyoshi Taisha Jindaiki|ja|住吉大社神代記|1=Sumiyoshi Taisha Shrine Chronicles|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi Taisha Jindaiki|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi Taisha Shrine Chronicles|qq=}} in the context of the Silla expedition.
== Analysis ==
Kagawake's existence is debated among scholars. Some argue he was a historical figure based on the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki|1=Nihon Shoki}}, genealogical records, and references in the {{ill|Baekje Annals|ja|百済記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baekje Annals|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 百済三書}}. Others suggest he is a later legendary addition{{Sfn|Kagawake (Ancient Clan)|2010}}.
The phonetic similarity of "Kaga" has led to theories linking Kagawake to {{ill|Ashikaga District, Tochigi|en|Ashikaga District, Tochigi|WD=Q11636831|lt=Ashikaga District|1=Ashikaga District, Tochigi}}, present-day {{ill|Ashikaga, Tochigi|en|Ashikaga, Tochigi|WD=Q374477|lt=Ashikaga|1=Ashikaga, Tochigi}}, Tochigi Prefecture. Some traditions suggest Kagawake is connected to the origin of the place name "Ashikaga." <ref>{{Cite book|title=Japanese Geographic Resources|chapter=Research on Keno Province in the Kofun Period}}</ref>
